text.NRSV: When I rose in the morning to nurse my son, I saw that he was dead; but when I looked at him closely in the morning, clearly it was not the son I had borne.”
text.Rotherham: And, when I arose in the morning, to give suck unto my child, lo! it was dead! but, when I looked at it narrowly in the morning, lo! it was not, my son that I had borne.
text.KJV: 3:21 And when I rose in the morning to giue my childe sucke, behold, it was dead: but when I had considered it in the morning, beholde, it was not my sonne, which I did beare.
text.NABRE: I rose in the morning to nurse my son, and he was dead! But when I examined him in the morning light, I saw it was not the son I had borne.”

text.NRSV: But the other woman said, “No, the living son is mine, and the dead son is yours.” The first said, “No, the dead son is yours, and the living son is mine.” So they argued before the king.
text.Rotherham: Then said the other woman—Nay! but, my son, is the, living, and, thy son, the, dead. But, this, one kept on saying—Nay, verily! but, thy son, is the, dead, and, my son, the, living. Thus spake they before the king.
text.KJV: 3:22 And the other woman said, Nay, but the liuing is my sonne, and the dead is thy sonne: And this said, No, but the dead is thy sonne, and the liuing is my sonne. Thus they spake before the king.
text.NABRE: The other woman answered, “No! The living one is my son, the dead one is yours.” But the first kept saying, “No! the dead one is your son, the living one is mine!” Thus they argued before the king.

text.NRSV: Then the king said, “The one says, ‘This is my son that is alive, and your son is dead’; while the other says, ‘No, your son is dead, and my son is the living one.’”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king, The one woman, is saying, This, is, my son, the one that liveth, and, thy son, is, the one that is dead,—and, the other, is saying, Nay! but, thy son, is, the dead one, and, my son, the, living.
text.KJV: 3:23 Then said the King, The one saith, This is my sonne, that liueth, and thy sonne is the dead: and the other saith Nay: but thy sonne is the dead, and my sonne is the liuing.
text.NABRE: Then the king said: “One woman claims, ‘This, the living one, is my son, the dead one is yours.’ The other answers, ‘No! The dead one is your son, the living one is mine.’”

text.NRSV: So the king said, “Bring me a sword,” and they brought a sword before the king.
text.Rotherham: And the king said—Bring me a sword! So they brought a sword before the king.
text.KJV: 3:24 And the King said, Bring mee a sword. And they brought a sword before the king:
text.NABRE: The king continued, “Get me a sword.” When they brought the sword before the king,

text.NRSV: The king said, “Divide the living boy in two; then give half to the one, and half to the other.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king, Divide the living child, in twain,—and give half to the one, and half to the other.
text.KJV: 3:25 And the king said, Diuide the liuing childe in two, and giue halfe to the one, and halfe to the other.
text.NABRE: he said, “Cut the living child in two, and give half to one woman and half to the other.”

text.NRSV: But the woman whose son was alive said to the king—because compassion for her son burned within her—“Please, my lord, give her the living boy; certainly do not kill him!” The other said, “It shall be neither mine nor yours; divide it.”
text.Rotherham: Then spake the woman, whose was the living child, unto the king—for tender became her compassions over her son—so she said—Pardon, my lord! Give, her, the living child, and do not, kill, it. But the other kept on saying—Neither mine, nor thine, shall it be, divide it.
text.KJV: 3:26 Then spake the woman whose the liuing childe was, vnto the king, (for her bowels yerned vpon her sonne) and she said, O my lord, giue her the liuing childe, and in no wise slay it: But the other said, Let it be neither mine nor thine, but diuide it.
text.NABRE: The woman whose son was alive, because she was stirred with compassion for her son, said to the king, “Please, my lord, give her the living baby—do not kill it!” But the other said, “It shall be neither mine nor yours. Cut it in two!”

text.NRSV: Then the king responded: “Give the first woman the living boy; do not kill him. She is his mother.”
text.Rotherham: Then responded the king, and said—Give, her, the living child, ye shall not, kill, it,—she, is its mother.
text.KJV: 3:27 Then the King answered and said, Giue her the liuing child, and in no wise slay it: she is the mother thereof.
text.NABRE: The king then answered, “Give her the living baby! Do not kill it! She is the mother.”

text.NRSV: All Israel heard of the judgment that the king had rendered; and they stood in awe of the king, because they perceived that the wisdom of God was in him, to execute justice.
text.Rotherham: And all Israel heard of the judgment which the king had delivered, and they stood in awe before the king,—for they saw, that, the wisdom of God, was in him, to execute justice.
text.KJV: 3:28 And all Israel heard of the Iudgement which the king had iudged, and they feared the King: for they saw that the wisedome of God was in him, to doe Iudgement.
text.NABRE: When all Israel heard the judgment the king had given, they were in awe of him, because they saw that the king had in him the wisdom of God for giving right judgment.

text.NRSV: King Solomon was king over all Israel,
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, that, King Solomon, was king, over all Israel.
text.KJV: 4:1 So King Solomon was king ouer all Israel.
text.NABRE: Chapter 4 Solomon’s Riches: Domestic Affairs. Solomon was king over all Israel,

text.NRSV: and these were his high officials: Azariah son of Zadok was the priest;
text.Rotherham: Now, these, were his chief officers of state,—Azariah son of Zadok, the priest;
text.KJV: 4:2 And these were the Princes which he had, Azariah the sonne of Zadok, the Priest,
text.NABRE: and these were the officials he had in his service: Azariah, son of Zadok, the priest;

text.NRSV: Elihoreph and Ahijah sons of Shisha were secretaries; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was recorder;
text.Rotherham: Elihoreph and Ahijah, sons of Shisha, scribes,—Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud, the recorder;
text.KJV: 4:3 Elihoreph, and Ahiah the sonnes of Shisha, Scribes: Iehoshaphat the sonne of Ahilud the Recorder;
text.NABRE: Elihoreph and Ahijah, sons of Shisha, scribes; Jehoshaphat, son of Ahilud, the chancellor;

text.NRSV: Benaiah son of Jehoiada was in command of the army; Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
text.Rotherham: And Benaiah son of Jehoiada, over the army,—and Zadok and Abiathar priests;
text.KJV: 4:4 And Benaiah the sonne of Iehoiada was ouer the host: And Zadok and Abiathar were the Priests:
text.NABRE: Benaiah, son of Jehoiada, in charge of the army; Zadok and Abiathar, priests;

text.NRSV: Azariah son of Nathan was over the officials; Zabud son of Nathan was priest and king’s friend;
text.Rotherham: And Azariah son of Nathan, over the governors,—and Zabud son of Nathan, minister, king’s friend;
text.KJV: 4:5 And Azariah the sonne of Nathan was ouer the officers: and Zabud the sonne of Nathan was principall officer, and the kings friend.
text.NABRE: Azariah, son of Nathan, in charge of the governors; Zabud, son of Nathan, priest and companion to the king;

text.NRSV: Ahishar was in charge of the palace; and Adoniram son of Abda was in charge of the forced labor.
text.Rotherham: And Ahishar, over the household,—and Adoniram son of Abda, over the tribute.
text.KJV: 4:6 And Ahishar was ouer the houshold: and Adoniram the sonne of Abda was ouer the tribute.
text.NABRE: Ahishar, master of the palace; and Adoniram, son of Abda, in charge of the forced labor.

text.NRSV: Solomon had twelve officials over all Israel, who provided food for the king and his household; each one had to make provision for one month in the year.
text.Rotherham: And, Solomon, had twelve governors over all Israel, who used to sustain the king and his household,—for a month in the year, was it appointed unto each one, to find sustenance.
text.KJV: 4:7 ¶ And Solomon had twelue officers ouer all Israel, which prouided victuals for the king and his houshold: each man his moneth in a yeere made prouision.
text.NABRE: Solomon had twelve governors over all Israel who supplied food for the king and his household, each having to provide for one month in the year.

text.NRSV: These were their names: Ben-hur, in the hill country of Ephraim;
text.Rotherham: And, these, are their names, Ben-hur, in the hill country of Ephraim;
text.KJV: 4:8 And these are their names: the sonne of Hur in mount Ephraim,
text.NABRE: Their names were: the son of Hur in the hill country of Ephraim;

text.NRSV: Ben-deker, in Makaz, Shaalbim, Beth-shemesh, and Elon-beth-hanan;
text.Rotherham: Ben-deker, in Makaz and in Shaalbim and Beth-shemesh,—and Elon-beth-hanan;
text.KJV: 4:9 The sonne of Dekar in Makaz, and in Shaalbim, and Bethshemesh, and Elon-Bethhanan.
text.NABRE: the son of Deker in Makaz, Shaalbim, Beth-shemesh, and Elon Beth-hanan;

text.NRSV: Ben-hesed, in Arubboth (to him belonged Socoh and all the land of Hepher);
text.Rotherham: Ben-hesed, in Arubboth,—to him, pertaineth Socoh, and all the land of Hepher;
text.KJV: 4:10 The sonne of Heseb in Aruboth, to him pertained Sochoh, and all the land of Hepher;
text.NABRE: the son of Hesed in Arubboth, as well as in Socoh and the whole region of Hepher;

text.NRSV: Ben-abinadab, in all Naphath-dor (he had Taphath, Solomon’s daughter, as his wife);
text.Rotherham: Ben-abinadab, all the height of Dor,—Taphath daughter of Solomon, had he to wife;
text.KJV: 4:11 The sonne of Abinadab in all the region of Dor, which had Taphath the daughter of Solomon to wife:
text.NABRE: the son of Abinadab, in all Naphath-dor; he was married to Taphath, Solomon’s daughter;

text.NRSV: Baana son of Ahilud, in Taanach, Megiddo, and all Beth-shean, which is beside Zarethan below Jezreel, and from Beth-shean to Abel-meholah, as far as the other side of Jokmeam;
text.Rotherham: Baana son of Ahilud, in Taanach, and Megiddo,—and all Beth-shean which is beside Zarethan, beneath Jezreel, from Beth-shean as far as Abel-meholah, as far as over against Jokmeam;
text.KJV: 4:12 Baana the sonne of Ahilud, to him pertained Taanach and Megiddo, and all Beth-shean, which is by Zartanah beneath Iezreel, from Beth-shean to Abel-Meholah, euen vnto the place that is beyond Iokneam:
text.NABRE: Baana, son of Ahilud, in Taanach and Megiddo and all Beth-shean near Zarethan below Jezreel, from Beth-shean to Abel-meholah to beyond Jokmeam;

text.NRSV: Ben-geber, in Ramoth-gilead (he had the villages of Jair son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead, and he had the region of Argob, which is in Bashan, sixty great cities with walls and bronze bars);
text.Rotherham: Ben-geber, in Ramoth-gilead,—to him, belonged the towns of Jair, son of Manasseh, which are in Gilead, his, was the region of Argob which is in Bashan, sixty great cities, with walls and with bars of bronze;
text.KJV: 4:13 The sonne of Geber in Ramoth Gilead, to him pertained the townes of Iair the sonne of Manasseh, which are in Gilead: to him also pertained the region of Argob, which is in Bashan, threescore great cities, with walles, and brasen barres.
text.NABRE: the son of Geber in Ramoth-gilead, having charge of the villages of Jair, son of Manasseh, in Gilead; and of the district of Argob in Bashan—sixty large walled cities with gates barred with bronze;

text.NRSV: Ahinadab son of Iddo, in Mahanaim;
text.Rotherham: Ahinadab son of Iddo, towards Mahanaim;
text.KJV: 4:14 Ahinadab the sonne of Iddo had Mahanaim.
text.NABRE: Ahinadab, son of Iddo, in Mahanaim;

text.NRSV: Ahimaaz, in Naphtali (he had taken Basemath, Solomon’s daughter, as his wife);
text.Rotherham: Ahimaaz, in Naphtali,—moreover, he, took Basemath daughter of Solomon, to wife;
text.KJV: 4:15 Ahimaaz was in Naphtali; he also tooke Basmath the daughter of Solomon to wife.
text.NABRE: Ahimaaz, in Naphtali; he was married to Basemath, another daughter of Solomon;

text.NRSV: Baana son of Hushai, in Asher and Bealoth;
text.Rotherham: Banna son of Hushai, in Asher and in Aloth;
text.KJV: 4:16 Baanah the sonne of Hushai was in Asher and in Aloth:
text.NABRE: Baana, son of Hushai, in Asher and Aloth;

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat son of Paruah, in Issachar;
text.Rotherham: Jehoshaphat son of Paruah, in Issachar;
text.KJV: 4:17 Iehoshaphat the sonne of Paruah in Issachar:
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat, son of Paruah, in Issachar;

text.NRSV: Shimei son of Ela, in Benjamin;
text.Rotherham: Shimei son of Ela, in Benjamin;
text.KJV: 4:18 Shimei the sonne of Elah in Beniamin:
text.NABRE: Shimei, son of Ela, in Benjamin;

text.NRSV: Geber son of Uri, in the land of Gilead, the country of King Sihon of the Amorites and of King Og of Bashan. And there was one official in the land of Judah.
text.Rotherham: Geber son of Uri, in the land of Gilead,—the land of Sihon king of the Amorites, and Og king of Bashan, being, the one governor, that was in the land.
text.KJV: 4:19 Geber the sonne of Uri was in the countrey of Gilead, in the countrey of Sihon king of the Amorites, and of Og king of Bashan; and hee was the onely officer which was in the land.
text.NABRE: Geber, son of Uri, in the land of Gilead, the land of Sihon, king of the Amorites, and of Og, king of Bashan. There was one governor besides, in the land of Judah.

text.NRSV: Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand by the sea; they ate and drank and were happy.
text.Rotherham: Judah and Israel, were many, as the sand that is by the sea, for multitude,—eating and drinking, and rejoicing.
text.KJV: 4:20 ¶ Iudah and Israel were many, as the sand which is by the sea in multitude, eating and drinking and making merrie.
text.NABRE: Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sands by the sea; they ate and drank and rejoiced.

text.NRSV: Solomon was sovereign over all the kingdoms from the Euphrates to the land of the Philistines, even to the border of Egypt; they brought tribute and served Solomon all the days of his life.
text.Rotherham: And, Solomon, became ruler over all the kingdoms, from the River [even unto] the land of the Philistines, and as far as the boundary of Egypt,—and these were bringing presents, and serving Solomon, all the days of his life.
text.KJV: 4:21 And Solomon reigned ouer all kingdoms from the riuer vnto the land of the Philistines, and vnto the border of Egypt: they brought presents, and serued Solomon all the dayes of his life.

text.NRSV: Solomon’s provision for one day was thirty cors of choice flour, and sixty cors of meal,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that Solomon’s provision for one day was,—thirty measures of fine flour, and sixty measures of meal;
text.KJV: 4:22 ¶ And Solomons prouision for one day, was thirtie measures of fine floure, and threescore measures of meale,

text.NRSV: ten fatted oxen, and twenty pasture-fed cattle, and one hundred sheep, besides deer, gazelles, roebucks, and fatted fowl.
text.Rotherham: ten fat oxen, and twenty oxen out of the pastures, and a hundred sheep,—besides harts, and gazelles, and roebucks, and fatted fowl.
text.KJV: 4:23 Ten fat oxen, and twentie oxen out of the pastures, and an hundred sheepe, beside Harts, and Roe-buckes, and fallow Deere, and fatted foule.

text.NRSV: For he had dominion over all the region west of the Euphrates from Tiphsah to Gaza, over all the kings west of the Euphrates; and he had peace on all sides fore and aft.
text.Rotherham: For, he, had dominion over all on this side the River, from Tiphsah even as far as Gaza, over all the kings on this side the River,—and he had, peace, on all sides of him, round about.
text.KJV: 4:24 For he had dominion ouer all the region on this side the Riuer, from Tiphsah euen to Azzah ouer all the kings on this side the Riuer: and he had peace on all sides round about him.

text.NRSV: During Solomon’s lifetime Judah and Israel lived in safety, from Dan even to Beer-sheba, all of them under their vines and fig trees.
text.Rotherham: And Judah and Israel dwelt securely, every man under his own vine, and under his own fig-tree, from Dan even unto Beer-sheba,—all the days of Solomon.
text.KJV: 4:25 And Iudah and Israel dwelt safely, euery man vnder his Uine, and vnder his Figtree, from Dan euen to Beer-sheba, all the dayes of Solomon.

text.NRSV: Solomon also had forty thousand stalls of horses for his chariots, and twelve thousand horsemen.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, Solomon, had forty thousand stalls of horses, for his chariots,—and twelve thousand horsemen.
text.KJV: 4:26 ¶ And Solomon had fourtie thousand stalles of horses for his charets, and twelue thousand horsemen.

text.NRSV: Those officials supplied provisions for King Solomon and for all who came to King Solomon’s table, each one in his month; they let nothing be lacking.
text.Rotherham: And these governors provided sustenance for King Solomon, and for all that drew near unto the table of King Solomon, every man in his month,—they let, nought, be lacking.
text.KJV: 4:27 And those officers prouided victuall for king Solomon, and for all that came vnto king Solomons table, euery man in his moneth: they lacked nothing.

text.NRSV: They also brought to the required place barley and straw for the horses and swift steeds, each according to his charge.
text.Rotherham: Barley also and crushed straw, for the horses and for the swift beasts, brought they in unto the place where it should be, every man according to his charge.
text.KJV: 4:28 Barley also and straw for the horses and dromedaries, brought they vnto the place where the officers were, euery man according to his charge.

text.NRSV: God gave Solomon very great wisdom, discernment, and breadth of understanding as vast as the sand on the seashore,
text.Rotherham: And God gave wisdom unto Solomon, and discernment, and very great largeness of heart,—like the sand that is on the shore of the sea:
text.KJV: 4:29 ¶ And God gaue Solomon wisdome, and vnderstanding, exceeding much, and largenesse of heart, euen as the sand that is on the sea shoare.

text.NRSV: so that Solomon’s wisdom surpassed the wisdom of all the people of the east, and all the wisdom of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: so that, the wisdom of Solomon, excelled, the wisdom of all the sons of the East,—and all the wisdom of the Egyptians,
text.KJV: 4:30 And Solomons wisedome excelled the wisedome of all the children of the East countrey, and all the wisedome of Egypt.

text.NRSV: He was wiser than anyone else, wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman, Calcol, and Darda, children of Mahol; his fame spread throughout all the surrounding nations.
text.Rotherham: so that he was wiser than any man—than Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman and Calcol and Darda, sons of Mahol,—and it came to pass that, his name, was throughout all the nations round about.
text.KJV: 4:31 For hee was wiser then all men; then Ethan the Ezrahite, and Heman, and Chalcol, and Darda the sonnes of Mahol: and his fame was in all nations round about.

text.NRSV: He composed three thousand proverbs, and his songs numbered a thousand and five.
text.Rotherham: And he spake three thousand proverbs,—and it came to pass that, his songs, were a thousand and five.
text.KJV: 4:32 And he spake three thousand prouerbes: and his songs were a thousand and fiue.

text.NRSV: He would speak of trees, from the cedar that is in the Lebanon to the hyssop that grows in the wall; he would speak of animals, and birds, and reptiles, and fish.
text.Rotherham: And he discoursed of trees—from the cedar that is in Lebanon, even unto the hyssop, that springeth out in the wall,—and he discoursed of beasts and of birds, and of creeping things, and of fishes.
text.KJV: 4:33 And hee spake of trees, from the Cedar tree that is in Lebanon, euen vnto the Hyssope that springeth out of the wall: hee spake also of beasts, and of foule, and of creeping things, and of fishes.

text.NRSV: People came from all the nations to hear the wisdom of Solomon; they came from all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom.
text.Rotherham: And there came in, of all the peoples, to hear the wisdom of Solomon—of all the kings of the earth, who heard his wisdom.
text.KJV: 4:34 And there came of all people to heare the wisedome of Solomon, from all kings of the earth, which had heard of his wisedome.

text.NRSV: Now King Hiram of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon, when he heard that they had anointed him king in place of his father; for Hiram had always been a friend to David.
text.Rotherham: And Hiram King of Tyre sent his servants unto Solomon, for he had heard that, him, had they anointed king, in the room of his father,—for, Hiram, was, a lover, of, David continually.
text.KJV: 5:1 And Hiram king of Tyre sent his seruants vnto Solomon: (for hee had heard that they had anointed him King in the roume of his father,) for Hiram was euer a louer of Dauid.
text.NABRE: Chapter 5 Solomon’s Riches: International Affairs. Solomon ruled over all the kingdoms from the River to the land of the Philistines, down to the border of Egypt; they paid Solomon tribute and served him as long as he lived.

text.NRSV: Solomon sent word to Hiram, saying,
text.Rotherham: So Solomon sent unto Hiram, saying:—
text.KJV: 5:2 And Solomon sent to Hiram, saying,
text.NABRE: Solomon’s provisions for each day were thirty kors of fine flour, sixty kors of meal,

text.NRSV: “You know that my father David could not build a house for the name of the Lord his God because of the warfare with which his enemies surrounded him, until the Lord put them under the soles of his feet.
text.Rotherham: Thou, knewest David my father, how that he could not build a house unto the Name of Yahweh his God, because of the wars that were about him on every side,—until Yahweh should put them under the soles of his feet;
text.KJV: 5:3 Thou knowest how that Dauid my father could not build an house vnto the Name of the Lord his God, for the warres which were about him on euery side, vntill the Lord put them vnder the soles of his feet.
text.NABRE: ten fatted oxen, twenty pasture-fed oxen, and a hundred sheep, not counting harts, gazelles, roebucks, and fatted fowl.

text.NRSV: But now the Lord my God has given me rest on every side; there is neither adversary nor misfortune.
text.Rotherham: but, now, Yahweh my God hath given me rest on every side,—there is neither adversary nor incident of evil.
text.KJV: 5:4 But now the Lord my God hath giuen me rest on euery side, so that there is neither aduersary, nor euill occurrent.
text.NABRE: He had dominion over all the land west of the River, from Tiphsah to Gaza, and all its kings, and he had peace on all his borders round about.

text.NRSV: So I intend to build a house for the name of the Lord my God, as the Lord said to my father David, ‘Your son, whom I will set on your throne in your place, shall build the house for my name.’
text.Rotherham: Behold me! then, purposing to build a house for the Name of Yahweh my God—even as Yahweh spake unto David my father, saying—Thy son, whom I will set in thy stead, upon thy throne, he, shall build the house for my Name.
text.KJV: 5:5 And behold, I purpose to build an house vnto the Name of the Lord my God, as the Lord spake vnto Dauid my father, saying, Thy sonne, whom I will set vpon thy throne in thy roume, he shall build an house vnto my Name.
text.NABRE: Thus Judah and Israel lived in security, everyone under their own vine and fig tree from Dan to Beer-sheba, as long as Solomon lived.

text.NRSV: Therefore command that cedars from the Lebanon be cut for me. My servants will join your servants, and I will give you whatever wages you set for your servants; for you know that there is no one among us who knows how to cut timber like the Sidonians.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, command thou that they fell me cedars, out of Lebanon, and, my servants, shall be, with thy servants, and, the hire of thy servants, will I give thee, according to all that thou shalt say,—for, thou, knowest, that, none amongst us, hath skill to fell timber, like the Zidonians.
text.KJV: 5:6 Now therefore command thou, that they hew me Cedar trees out of Lebanon, and my seruants shall bee with thy seruants: and vnto thee will I giue hire for thy seruants, according to all that thou shalt appoint: for thou knowest that there is not among vs, any that can skill to hew timber, like vnto the Sidonians.
text.NABRE: Solomon’s Riches: Chariots and Horses. Solomon had forty thousand stalls for horses for chariots and twelve thousand horsemen.

text.NRSV: When Hiram heard the words of Solomon, he rejoiced greatly, and said, “Blessed be the Lord today, who has given to David a wise son to be over this great people.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Hiram heard the words of Solomon, that he rejoiced greatly,—and said: Blessed, be Yahweh, to-day, who hath given unto David a wise son, over this great people!
text.KJV: 5:7 ¶ And it came to passe when Hiram heard the wordes of Solomon, that hee reioyced greatly, and said, Blessed be the Lord this day, which hath giuen vnto Dauid a wise sonne ouer this great people.
text.NABRE: The governors, one for each month, provided food for King Solomon and for all the guests at King Solomon’s table. They left nothing unprovided.

text.NRSV: Hiram sent word to Solomon, “I have heard the message that you have sent to me; I will fulfill all your needs in the matter of cedar and cypress timber.
text.Rotherham: And Hiram sent unto Solomon, saying, I have heard that which thou hast sent unto me,—I, will do all thy desire, as to timber of cedar, and timber of fir.
text.KJV: 5:8 And Hiram sent to Solomon, saying, I haue considered the things which thou sentest to me for: and I will doe all thy desire concerning timber of Cedar, and concerning timber of firre.
text.NABRE: For the chariot horses and draft animals also, each brought his quota of barley and straw to the required place.

text.NRSV: My servants shall bring it down to the sea from the Lebanon; I will make it into rafts to go by sea to the place you indicate. I will have them broken up there for you to take away. And you shall meet my needs by providing food for my household.”
text.Rotherham: My servants, shall bring them down out of Lebanon unto the sea, and, I, will put them in rafts on the sea, unto the place of which thou shalt send me word, and will cause them to be discharged there, and, thou, shalt receive them, and, thou, shalt do my desire, by giving food for my household.
text.KJV: 5:9 My seruants shall bring them downe from Lebanon vnto the Sea: and I wil conuey them by sea in flotes, vnto the place that thou shalt appoint me, and will cause them to be discharged there, and thou shalt receiue them: and thou shalt accomplish my desire, in giuing food for my houshold.
text.NABRE: Solomon’s Renown. Moreover, God gave Solomon wisdom, exceptional understanding, and knowledge, as vast as the sand on the seashore.

text.NRSV: So Hiram supplied Solomon’s every need for cedar and cypress timber.
text.Rotherham: So then Hiram began giving unto Solomon timber of cedar, and timber of fir, even all his desire,
text.KJV: 5:10 So Hiram gaue Solomon Cedar trees, and Firre trees, according to all his desire.
text.NABRE: Solomon’s wisdom surpassed that of all the peoples of the East and all the wisdom of Egypt.

text.NRSV: Solomon in turn gave Hiram twenty thousand cors of wheat as food for his household, and twenty cors of fine oil. Solomon gave this to Hiram year by year.
text.Rotherham: and, Solomon, gave unto Hiram, twenty thousand measures of wheat, as food for his household, and twenty measures of beaten oil,—thus, used Solomon on to give unto Hiram, year by year.
text.KJV: 5:11 And Solomon gaue Hiram twentie thousand measures of wheate for food to his houshold, and twentie measures of pure oile: thus gaue Solomon to Hiram yeere by yeere.
text.NABRE: He was wiser than anyone else—wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite, or Heman, Chalcol, and Darda, the musicians—and his fame spread throughout the neighboring peoples.

text.NRSV: The Lord gave Solomon wisdom, as he promised him. There was peace between Hiram and Solomon; and the two of them made a treaty.
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, gave wisdom unto Solomon, as he promised him,—and it came to pass that there was peace between Hiram and Solomon, and, they two, solemnised a covenant.
text.KJV: 5:12 And the Lord gaue Solomon wisedome, as hee promised him: and there was peace betweene Hiram and Solomon, and they two made a league together.
text.NABRE: Solomon also uttered three thousand proverbs, and his songs numbered a thousand and five.

text.NRSV: King Solomon conscripted forced labor out of all Israel; the levy numbered thirty thousand men.
text.Rotherham: And King Solomon raised a labour-band, out of all Israel,—and it came to pass that, the labour-band, was thirty thousand men.
text.KJV: 5:13 ¶ And King Solomon raised a leuie out of all Israel, and the leuie was thirtie thousand men.
text.NABRE: He spoke of plants, from the cedar on Lebanon to the hyssop growing out of the wall, and he spoke about beasts, birds, reptiles, and fishes.

text.NRSV: He sent them to the Lebanon, ten thousand a month in shifts; they would be a month in the Lebanon and two months at home; Adoniram was in charge of the forced labor.
text.Rotherham: And he sent them to Lebanon, ten thousand a month, by courses, a month, were they in Lebanon, two months, at home,—and, Adoniram, was over the levy.
text.KJV: 5:14 And hee sent them to Lebanon, ten thousand a moneth by courses: a moneth they were in Lebanon, and two moneths at home: and Adoniram was ouer the leuie.
text.NABRE: People from all nations came to hear Solomon’s wisdom, sent by all the kings of the earth who had heard of his wisdom. Preparations for the Temple.

text.NRSV: Solomon also had seventy thousand laborers and eighty thousand stonecutters in the hill country,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, Solomon, had seventy thousand bearers of burdens,—and eighty thousand hewers in the mountain;
text.KJV: 5:15 And Solomon had threescore and ten thousand that bare burdens, and fourescore thousand hewers in the mountaines:
text.NABRE: When Hiram, king of Tyre, heard that Solomon had been anointed king in place of his father, he sent an embassy to him; for Hiram had always been David’s friend.

text.NRSV: besides Solomon’s three thousand three hundred supervisors who were over the work, having charge of the people who did the work.
text.Rotherham: besides the chiefs of Solomon’s officers, who were over the work, three thousand and three hundred,—who ruled over the people that wrought in the work.
text.KJV: 5:16 Besides the chiefe of Solomons officers which were ouer the worke, three thousand and three hundred, which ruled ouer the people that wrought in the worke.
text.NABRE: Solomon sent back this message to Hiram:

text.NRSV: At the king’s command, they quarried out great, costly stones in order to lay the foundation of the house with dressed stones.
text.Rotherham: And the king commanded, and they quarried great stones, costly stones, to found the house with hewn stones.
text.KJV: 5:17 And the king commanded, and they brought great stones, costly stones, and hewed stones, to lay the foundation of the house.
text.NABRE: “You know that David my father, because of the wars that beset him, could not build a house for the name of the Lord his God until such time as the Lord should put his enemies under the soles of his feet.

text.NRSV: So Solomon’s builders and Hiram’s builders and the Gebalites did the stonecutting and prepared the timber and the stone to build the house.
text.Rotherham: And Solomon’s builders and Hiram’s builders with the Gebalites wrought them,—thus made they ready the timber and the stones, for building the house.
text.KJV: 5:18 And Solomons builders, and Hirams builders, did hewe them, and the stone-squarers: so they prepared timber and stones to build the house.
text.NABRE: But now the Lord, my God, has given me rest on all sides, without adversary or misfortune.

text.NRSV: In the four hundred eightieth year after the Israelites came out of the land of Egypt, in the fourth year of Solomon’s reign over Israel, in the month of Ziv, which is the second month, he began to build the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, in the four hundred and eightieth year, by the coming forth of the sons of Israel out of the land of Egypt, in the fourth year (in the month of Zif, the same, being the second month) of the reign of Solomon over Israel, that he began to build the house unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: 6:1 And it came to passe in the foure hundred and fourescore yeere after the children of Israel were come out of the land of Egypt, in the fourth yere of Solomons reigne ouer Israel, in the moneth Zif, which is the second moneth, that he began to build the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Chapter 6 Building of the Temple. In the four hundred and eightieth year after the Israelites went forth from the land of Egypt, in the fourth year of Solomon’s reign over Israel, in the month of Ziv (the second month), he began to build the house of the Lord.

text.NRSV: The house that King Solomon built for the Lord was sixty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high.
text.Rotherham: Now, the house which King Solomon built unto Yahweh, was sixty cubits in length, and twenty in breadth,—and thirty cubits in height.
text.KJV: 6:2 And the house which king Solomon built for the Lord, the length thereof was threescore cubites, and the breadth thereof twentie cubits, and the height thereof thirtie cubites.
text.NABRE: The house which King Solomon built for the Lord was sixty cubits long, twenty wide, and thirty high.

text.NRSV: The vestibule in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits wide, corresponding to the width of the house, and its depth was ten cubits in front of the house.
text.Rotherham: And, the porch in front of the temple of the house, was twenty cubits in length, according to the breadth of the house,—ten cubits in breadth, in front of the house.
text.KJV: 6:3 And the porch before the Temple of the house: twentie cubites was the length thereof, according to the breadth of the house, and tenne cubites was the breadth thereof before the house.
text.NABRE: The porch in front of the nave of the house was twenty cubits from side to side along the width of the house, and ten cubits deep in front of the house.

text.NRSV: For the house he made windows with recessed frames.
text.Rotherham: And he made to the house windows, with closed bars.
text.KJV: 6:4 And for the house he made windowes of narrow lights.
text.NABRE: Windows with closed lattices were made for the house,

text.NRSV: He also built a structure against the wall of the house, running around the walls of the house, both the nave and the inner sanctuary; and he made side chambers all around.
text.Rotherham: And he built—by the wall of the house—a gallery, round about, against the walls of the house, round about, both to the temple, and to the shrine,—and he made side chambers round about.
text.KJV: 6:5 ¶ And against the wall of the house he built chambers round about, against the walles of the house round about, both of the Temple and of the Oracle: and hee made chambers round about.
text.NABRE: and adjoining the wall of the house he built a substructure around its walls that enclosed the nave and the inner sanctuary, and he made side chambers all around.

text.NRSV: The lowest story was five cubits wide, the middle one was six cubits wide, and the third was seven cubits wide; for around the outside of the house he made offsets on the wall in order that the supporting beams should not be inserted into the walls of the house.
text.Rotherham: The, lowest gallery, was five cubits broad, and, the middle, six cubits broad, and, the third, seven cubits broad,—for he put, ledges, against the house round about, on the outer side, so as not to make fastenings in the walls of the house.
text.KJV: 6:6 The nethermost chamber was fiue cubites broad, and the middle was sixe cubites broad, and the third was seuen cubites broad: for without in the wall of the house hee made narrowed rests round about, that the beames should not bee fastened in the walles of the house.
text.NABRE: The lowest story was five cubits wide, the middle one six cubits wide, the third seven cubits wide, because he put recesses along the outside of the house to avoid fastening anything into the walls of the house.

text.NRSV: The house was built with stone finished at the quarry, so that neither hammer nor ax nor any tool of iron was heard in the house while it was being built.
text.Rotherham: Now, the house, when it was in building, with whole quarry-stones, was built,—neither hammer, nor axe, nor any tools of iron, was heard in the house, when it was in building.
text.KJV: 6:7 And the house when it was in building, was built of stone, made ready before it was brought thither: so that there was neither hammer nor axe, nor any toole of yron heard in the house, while it was in building.
text.NABRE: The house was built of stone dressed at the quarry, so that no hammer or ax, no iron tool, was to be heard in the house during its construction.

text.NRSV: The entrance for the middle story was on the south side of the house: one went up by winding stairs to the middle story, and from the middle story to the third.
text.Rotherham: The entrance of the lowest story, was on the right side of the house,—and, by winding stairs, went they up unto the middle [story], and, out of the middle, into, the third.
text.KJV: 6:8 The doore for the middle chamber was in the right side of the house: and they went vp with winding staires into the middle chamber, and out of the middle into the third.
text.NABRE: The entrance to the middle story was on the south side of the house; stairs led up to the middle story and from the middle story to the third.

text.NRSV: So he built the house, and finished it; he roofed the house with beams and planks of cedar.
text.Rotherham: So he built the house, and finished it,—and ceiled the house with planks and beams of cedar;
text.KJV: 6:9 So he built the house and finished it: and couered the house with beams and boards of Cedar.
text.NABRE: When he had finished building the house, it was roofed in with rafters and boards of cedar.

text.NRSV: He built the structure against the whole house, each story five cubits high, and it was joined to the house with timbers of cedar.
text.Rotherham: and built the gallery against all the house, five cubits in height,—and so fastened the house, with timber of cedar.
text.KJV: 6:10 And then hee built chambers against all the house, fiue cubites high: and they rested on the house with timber of Cedar.
text.NABRE: He built the substructure five cubits high all along the outside of the house, to which it was joined by cedar beams.

text.NRSV: Now the word of the Lord came to Solomon,
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto Solomon, saying:
text.KJV: 6:11 ¶ And the word of the Lord came to Solomon, saying;
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord came to Solomon:

text.NRSV: “Concerning this house that you are building, if you will walk in my statutes, obey my ordinances, and keep all my commandments by walking in them, then I will establish my promise with you, which I made to your father David.
text.Rotherham: Concerning this house, which thou art building, If thou wilt walk in my statutes, and, my regulations, wilt practise, and so observe all my commandments, by walking in them, Then will I establish my word with thee, which I spake unto David thy father;
text.KJV: 6:12 Concerning this House which thou art in building, if thou wilt walke in my Statutes, and execute my Iudgments, and keepe all my Commandements to walke in them: then will I performe my word with thee, which I spake vnto Dauid thy father.
text.NABRE: As to this house you are building—if you walk in my statutes, carry out my ordinances, and observe all my commands, walking in them, I will fulfill toward you my word which I spoke to David your father.

text.NRSV: I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will not forsake my people Israel.”
text.Rotherham: and will make my habitation in the midst of the sons of Israel,—and will not forsake my people Israel.
text.KJV: 6:13 And I will dwell among the children of Israel, and will not forsake my people Israel.
text.NABRE: I will dwell in the midst of the Israelites and will not forsake my people Israel.

text.NRSV: So Solomon built the house, and finished it.
text.Rotherham: Thus Solomon built the house, and finished it;
text.KJV: 6:14 So Solomon built the house, and finished it.
text.NABRE: When Solomon finished building the house,

text.NRSV: He lined the walls of the house on the inside with boards of cedar; from the floor of the house to the rafters of the ceiling, he covered them on the inside with wood; and he covered the floor of the house with boards of cypress.
text.Rotherham: and he built the walls of the house on the inside, with boards of cedar, from the floor of the house, unto the cross-beams of the ceiling, he overlaid it with wood, on the inside,—and he overlaid the floor of the house with boards of fir.
text.KJV: 6:15 And hee built the walles of the house within with boards of Cedar, both the floore of the house, and the walles of the sieling: and hee couered them on the inside with wood, and couered the floore of the house with plankes of firre.
text.NABRE: its inside walls were lined with cedar paneling: he covered the interior with wood from floor to ceiling, and he covered its floor with fir planking.

text.NRSV: He built twenty cubits of the rear of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the rafters, and he built this within as an inner sanctuary, as the most holy place.
text.Rotherham: And he built twenty cubits, at the hinder part of the house, with boards of cedar, from the floor as far as the cross-beams,—and he built it within, for a shrine, even for the holy of holies;
text.KJV: 6:16 And hee built twentie cubites on the sides of the house, both the floore, and the walles with boards of Cedar: he euen built them for it within, euen for the Oracle, euen for the most holy place.
text.NABRE: At the rear of the house a space of twenty cubits was set off by cedar panels from the floor to the ceiling, enclosing the inner sanctuary, the holy of holies.

text.NRSV: The house, that is, the nave in front of the inner sanctuary, was forty cubits long.
text.Rotherham: but, forty cubits, was the house itself,—the same, was the temple, before the oracle.
text.KJV: 6:17 And the house, that is, the Temple before it, was fortie cubites long.
text.NABRE: The house was forty cubits long, that is, the nave, the part in front.

text.NRSV: The cedar within the house was carved with gourds and open flowers; all was cedar, no stone was seen.
text.Rotherham: And, the cedar for the house within, was carved with colocynths, and with festoons of flowers,—the whole, was cedar, there was no stone to be seen.
text.KJV: 6:18 And the Cedar of the house within was carued with knops, and open flowres: all was Cedar, there was no stone seene.
text.NABRE: The cedar in the interior of the house was carved in the form of gourds and open flowers; all was of cedar, and no stone was to be seen.

text.NRSV: The inner sanctuary he prepared in the innermost part of the house, to set there the ark of the covenant of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, the shrine in the midst of the house within, made he ready,—for placing there, the ark of the covenant of Yahweh.
text.KJV: 6:19 And the Oracle he prepared in the house within, to set there the Arke of the Couenant of the Lord.
text.NABRE: In the innermost part of the house he set up the inner sanctuary to house the ark of the Lord ’s covenant.

text.NRSV: The interior of the inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long, twenty cubits wide, and twenty cubits high; he overlaid it with pure gold. He also overlaid the altar with cedar.
text.Rotherham: And, the interior of the shrine, was twenty cubits in length, and twenty cubits in breadth, and twenty cubits in height, and he overlaid it, with pure gold,—and overlaid the altar with cedar.
text.KJV: 6:20 And the Oracle in the forepart, was twenty cubits in length and twentie cubites in breadth, and twentie cubites in the height thereof: and hee ouerlayd it with pure golde, and so couered the Altar which was of Cedar.
text.NABRE: In front of the inner sanctuary (it was twenty cubits long, twenty wide, and twenty high, and he covered it with pure gold), he made an altar of cedar.

text.NRSV: Solomon overlaid the inside of the house with pure gold, then he drew chains of gold across, in front of the inner sanctuary, and overlaid it with gold.
text.Rotherham: Thus then did Solomon overlay the house within, with pure gold,—and he drew chains of gold across the front of the shrine, and overlaid it with gold;
text.KJV: 6:21 So Solomon ouerlayd the house within with pure golde: and he made a partition, by the chaines of golde before the Oracle, and he ouerlaid it with gold.
text.NABRE: Solomon covered the interior of the house with pure gold, and he drew golden chains across in front of the inner sanctuary, and covered it with gold.

text.NRSV: Next he overlaid the whole house with gold, in order that the whole house might be perfect; even the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary he overlaid with gold.
text.Rotherham: yea, all the house, overlaid he with gold, until he had made all the house into one whole,—even all the altar that was by the shrine, overlaid he with gold.
text.KJV: 6:22 And the whole house he ouerlaid with golde vntill he had finished all the house: also the whole Altar that was by the Oracle he ouerlaide with golde.
text.NABRE: He covered the whole house with gold, until the whole house was done, and the whole altar that belonged to the inner sanctuary he covered with gold.

text.NRSV: In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim of olivewood, each ten cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And he made, within the shrine, two cherubim of wild olive wood,—ten cubits in height;
text.KJV: 6:23 ¶ And within the Oracle he made two Cherubims of Oliue tree, each ten cubites high.
text.NABRE: In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim, each ten cubits high, made of pine.

text.NRSV: Five cubits was the length of one wing of the cherub, and five cubits the length of the other wing of the cherub; it was ten cubits from the tip of one wing to the tip of the other.
text.Rotherham: and, five cubits, was the one wing of the cherub, and, five cubits, the second wing of the cherub,—ten cubits, from the one end of his wings unto the other end of his wings.
text.KJV: 6:24 And fiue cubits was the one wing of the Cherub, and fiue cubits the other wing of the Cherub: from the vttermost part of the one wing, vnto the vttermost part of the other, were ten cubites.
text.NABRE: Each wing of a cherub was five cubits so that the span from wing tip to wing tip was ten cubits.

text.NRSV: The other cherub also measured ten cubits; both cherubim had the same measure and the same form.
text.Rotherham: Ten cubits, also was the second cherub,—of one size and one shape, were the two cherubim.
text.KJV: 6:25 And the other Cherub was tenne cubites: both the Cherubims were of one measure, and one size.
text.NABRE: The second cherub was also ten cubits: the two cherubim were identical in size and shape;

text.NRSV: The height of one cherub was ten cubits, and so was that of the other cherub.
text.Rotherham: The height of the one cherub, was ten cubits,—and, so, of the second cherub.
text.KJV: 6:26 The height of the one Cherub was ten cubites, and so was it of the other Cherub.
text.NABRE: the first cherub was ten cubits high, and so was the second.

text.NRSV: He put the cherubim in the innermost part of the house; the wings of the cherubim were spread out so that a wing of one was touching one wall, and a wing of the other cherub was touching the other wall; their other wings met in the center of the house, wing to wing.
text.Rotherham: And he placed the cherubim in the midst of the inner house, and the cherubim stretched forth their wings,—so that the wing of the one touched the [one] wall, and the wing of the other cherub, was touching the other wall,—and, their wings in the midst of the house, were touching, wing to wing.
text.KJV: 6:27 And he set the Cherubims within the inner house: and they stretched foorth the wings of the Cherubims, so that the wing of the one touched the one wall, and the wing of the other Cherub touched the other wall: & their wings touched one another in the midst of the house.
text.NABRE: He placed the cherubim in the inmost part of the house; the wings of the cherubim were spread wide, so that one wing of the first touched the side wall and the wing of the second touched the other wall; the wings pointing to the middle of the room touched each other.

text.NRSV: He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
text.Rotherham: And he overlaid the cherubim with gold.
text.KJV: 6:28 And he ouerlayd the Cherubims with golde.
text.NABRE: He overlaid the cherubim with gold.

text.NRSV: He carved the walls of the house all around about with carved engravings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, in the inner and outer rooms.
text.Rotherham: And, all the walls of the house round about, carved he with figures of cherubim, and palm-trees, and festoons of flowers,—within and without.
text.KJV: 6:29 And he carued all the walles of the house round about with carued figures of Cherubims, and palme trees, and open flowers, within & without.
text.NABRE: The walls of the house on all sides of both the inner and the outer rooms had carved figures of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers.

text.NRSV: The floor of the house he overlaid with gold, in the inner and outer rooms.
text.Rotherham: Even the floor of the house, overlaid he with gold,—within and without.
text.KJV: 6:30 And the floore of the house hee ouerlayed with gold, within and without.
text.NABRE: The floor of the house of both the inner and the outer rooms was overlaid with gold.

text.NRSV: For the entrance to the inner sanctuary he made doors of olivewood; the lintel and the doorposts were five-sided.
text.Rotherham: And, the entrance of the shrine, made he with doors, of wild olive-wood, the pillars and posts being a fifth.
text.KJV: 6:31 ¶ And for the entring of the Oracle he made doores of Oliue tree: the lintell and side posts were a fifth part of the wall.
text.NABRE: At the entrance of the inner sanctuary, doors of pine were made; the doorframes had five-sided posts.

text.NRSV: He covered the two doors of olivewood with carvings of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers; he overlaid them with gold, and spread gold on the cherubim and on the palm trees.
text.Rotherham: The two doors, also were of olive-wood, and he carved upon them carvings of cherubim, and palm-trees, and festoons of flowers, and overlaid them with gold,—yea he spread out, over the cherubim and over the palm-trees, the gold.
text.KJV: 6:32 The two doores also were of Oliue tree, and he carued vpon them caruings of Cherubims, and palme trees, and open flowers, and ouerlayd them with gold, and spread gold vpon the Cherubims, and vpon the palme trees.
text.NABRE: The two doors were of pine, with carved figures of cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers. The doors were overlaid with gold, and the cherubim and the palm trees were also covered with beaten gold.

text.NRSV: So also he made for the entrance to the nave doorposts of olivewood, four-sided,
text.Rotherham: So, also, made he for the entrance of the temple, with posts of wild olive-wood,—a fourth part.
text.KJV: 6:33 So also made hee for the doore of the Temple postes of Oliue tree a fourth part of the wall.
text.NABRE: He did the same at the entrance to the nave, where the doorposts were of pine and were four-sided.

text.NRSV: and two doors of cypress wood; the two leaves of the one door were folding, and the two leaves of the other door were folding.
text.Rotherham: And, the two doors, were of fir wood,—the two leaves of the one door, were folding, and, the two leaves of the other door, were folding.
text.KJV: 6:34 And the two doores were of firre tree: the two leaues of the one doore were folding, and the two leaues of the other doore were folding.
text.NABRE: The two doors were of fir wood, each door consisting of two panels hinged together;

text.NRSV: He carved cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, overlaying them with gold evenly applied upon the carved work.
text.Rotherham: And he carved cherubim, and palm-trees, and festoons of flowers, and overlaid them with gold, smoothed out on the carved work.
text.KJV: 6:35 And he carued thereon Cherubims, and palme trees, and open flowers: and couered them with gold, fitted vpon the carued worke.
text.NABRE: and he carved cherubim, palm trees, and open flowers, and plated them with gold.

text.NRSV: He built the inner court with three courses of dressed stone to one course of cedar beams.
text.Rotherham: And he built the inner court, with three rows of hewn stone,—and a row of beams of cedar.
text.KJV: 6:36 ¶ And hee built the inner Court with three rowes of hewed stone, and a row of Cedar beames.
text.NABRE: He walled off the inner court with three courses of hewn stones and one course of cedar beams.

text.NRSV: In the fourth year the foundation of the house of the Lord was laid, in the month of Ziv.
text.Rotherham: In the fourth year, was laid the foundation of the house of Yahweh,—in the month Zif;
text.KJV: 6:37 ¶ In the fourth yeere was the foundation of the house of the Lord layd, in the moneth Zif.
text.NABRE: The foundations of the Lord ’s house were laid in the month of Ziv in the fourth year,

text.NRSV: In the eleventh year, in the month of Bul, which is the eighth month, the house was finished in all its parts, and according to all its specifications. He was seven years in building it.
text.Rotherham: and, in the eleventh year, in the month Bul—the same, is the eighth month, was the house finished, as to all the particulars thereof, and as to all the appointments thereof,—so then he built it in seven years.
text.KJV: 6:38 And in the eleuenth yeere in the moneth Bul (which is the eight moneth) was the house finished throughout all the parts therof, and according to all the fashion of it: So was he seuen yeeres in building it.
text.NABRE: and it was finished, in all particulars, exactly according to plan, in the month of Bul, the eighth month, in the eleventh year. Thus Solomon built it in seven years.

text.NRSV: Solomon was building his own house thirteen years, and he finished his entire house.
text.Rotherham: But, his own house, was Solomon thirteen years in building,—so he finished all his house.
text.KJV: 7:1 But Solomon was building his owne house thirteene yeres, and he finished all his house.
text.NABRE: Chapter 7 To finish the building of his own house Solomon took thirteen years.

text.NRSV: He built the House of the Forest of the Lebanon one hundred cubits long, fifty cubits wide, and thirty cubits high, built on four rows of cedar pillars, with cedar beams on the pillars.
text.Rotherham: Yea he built the house of the forest of Lebanon, a hundred cubits, the length thereof, and, fifty cubits, the breadth thereof, and, thirty cubits, the height thereof,—upon four rows of pillars of cedar, with beams of cedar, upon the pillars;
text.KJV: 7:2 ¶ Hee built also the house of the forrest of Lebanon; the length thereof was a hundred cubites, and the breadth thereof fiftie cubites, and the height thereof thirtie cubites, vpon foure rowes of Cedar pillars, with Cedar beames vpon the pillars.
text.NABRE: He built the House of the Forest of Lebanon one hundred cubits long, fifty wide, and thirty high; it was supported by four rows of cedar columns, with cedar beams upon the columns.

text.NRSV: It was roofed with cedar on the forty-five rafters, fifteen in each row, which were on the pillars.
text.Rotherham: and it was covered with cedar above upon the joists, that were on forty-five pillars,—fifteen in a row.
text.KJV: 7:3 And it was couered with Cedar aboue vpon the beames, that lay on fortie fiue pillars, fifteene in a row.
text.NABRE: Moreover, it had a ceiling of cedar above the rafters resting on the columns; these rafters numbered forty-five, fifteen to a row.

text.NRSV: There were window frames in three rows, facing each other in the three rows.
text.Rotherham: And there were, window spaces, in three rows,—and light over against light, three times.
text.KJV: 7:4 And there were windowes in three rowes, and light was against light in three rankes.
text.NABRE: There were lattices in three rows, each row facing the next,

text.NRSV: All the doorways and windows had square frames, and they faced each other in three rows.
text.Rotherham: And, all the openings and the posts, were square in their frame,—and light was over against light, three times.
text.KJV: 7:5 And all the doores and postes were square, with the windowes: and light was against light in three rankes.
text.NABRE: and all the openings and doorposts were squared with lintels, each facing across from the next.

text.NRSV: He made the Hall of Pillars fifty cubits long and thirty cubits wide. There was a porch in front with pillars, and a canopy in front of them.
text.Rotherham: And, a porch of pillars, made he, fifty cubits, in length, and, thirty cubits, in breadth,—and, a porch, was on the one front of them, and pillars and threshold, were on the other front of them.
text.KJV: 7:6 ¶ And he made a porch of pillars, the length thereof was fiftie cubites, and the breadth thereof thirtie cubites: and the porch was before them: and the other pillars, and the thicke beame were before them.
text.NABRE: He also made the Porch of Columns, fifty cubits long and thirty wide. The porch extended across the front, and there were columns with a canopy in front of them.

text.NRSV: He made the Hall of the Throne where he was to pronounce judgment, the Hall of Justice, paneled with cedar from floor to floor.
text.Rotherham: And, a porch for the throne, where he should judge, even the porch of judgment, made he, and it was wainscotted with cedar, from floor to ceiling.
text.KJV: 7:7 ¶ Then hee made a porch for the throne where he might iudge, euen the porch of Iudgement: and it was couered with Cedar from one side of the floore to the other.
text.NABRE: He also made the Porch of the Throne where he gave judgment—that is, the Porch of Judgment; it was paneled with cedar from floor to ceiling beams.

text.NRSV: His own house where he did live, in the other court back of the hall, was of like workmanship. Solomon also made a house like this hall for Pharaoh’s daughter, whom he had taken in marriage.
text.Rotherham: And, his own house where he should dwell, in the other court within the porch, was, of like workmanship unto this,—a house also, made he, for the daughter of Pharaoh, whom Solomon had taken [to wife], in a similar porch.
text.KJV: 7:8 ¶ And his house where he dwelt, had another court within the porch, which was of the like worke: Solomon made also an house for Pharaohs daughter, ( whom he had taken to wife) like vnto this porch.
text.NABRE: The house in which he lived was in another court, set in deeper than the Porch and of the same construction. (Solomon made a house like this Porch for Pharaoh’s daughter, whom he had married.)

text.NRSV: All these were made of costly stones, cut according to measure, sawed with saws, back and front, from the foundation to the coping, and from outside to the great court.
text.Rotherham: All these, were of costly stones, after the dimensions of hewn stones, sawn with saws, within and without,—even from the foundation, unto the coping, and without as far as the great court.
text.KJV: 7:9 All these were of costly stones, according to the measures of hewed stones, sawed with sawes, within and without, euen from the foundation vnto the coping, and so on the outside toward the great court.
text.NABRE: All these buildings were of fine stones, hewn to size and trimmed front and back with a saw, from the foundation to the bonding course and outside as far as the great court.

text.NRSV: The foundation was of costly stones, huge stones, stones of eight and ten cubits.
text.Rotherham: And, the foundation, was of costly stones, large stones, stones of ten cubits, and stones of eight cubits.
text.KJV: 7:10 And the foundation was of costly stones, euen great stones; stones of ten cubites, and stones of eight cubites.
text.NABRE: The foundation was made of fine, large blocks, some ten cubits and some eight cubits.

text.NRSV: Above were costly stones, cut according to measure, and cedar.
text.Rotherham: And, above, were costly stones, after the dimensions of hewn stone, and cedar.
text.KJV: 7:11 And aboue were costly stones (after the measures of hewed stones) and Cedars.
text.NABRE: Above were fine stones hewn to size, and cedar wood.

text.NRSV: The great court had three courses of dressed stone to one course of cedar beams all around; so had the inner court of the house of the Lord, and the vestibule of the house.
text.Rotherham: And, the great court round about, was of three rows of hewn stone, and a row of beams of cedar,—both to the inner court of the house of Yahweh, and to the porch of the house.
text.KJV: 7:12 And the great court round about, was with three rowes of hewed stones, and a row of Cedar beames, both for the inner court of the house of the Lord, and for the porch of the house.
text.NABRE: The great court had three courses of hewn stones all around and a course of cedar beams. So also were the inner court of the house of the Lord and its porch.

text.NRSV: Now King Solomon sent and brought Hiram from Tyre.
text.Rotherham: And King Solomon sent and fetched Hiram, out of Tyre:
text.KJV: 7:13 ¶ And king Solomon sent and fet Hiram out of Tyre.
text.NABRE: King Solomon brought Hiram from Tyre.

text.NRSV: He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali, whose father, a Tyrian, had been a worker in bronze; he was full of skill, intelligence, and knowledge in working any work in bronze. He came to King Solomon, and did all his work.
text.Rotherham: son of a widow woman, was he of the tribe of Naphtali, but, his father, was a man of Tyre, a worker in bronze, and he was filled with wisdom, and discernment, and knowledge, for executing all manner of work in bronze,—so he came unto King Solomon, and executed all his work.
text.KJV: 7:14 Hee was a widowes sonne of the tribe of Naphtali, and his father was a man of Tyre, a worker in brasse, and he was filled with wisedome, and vnderstanding, and cunning to worke all workes in brasse: and hee came to king Solomon, and wrought all his worke.
text.NABRE: He was a bronze worker, the son of a widow from the tribe of Naphtali; his father had been from Tyre. He was endowed with wisdom, understanding, and knowledge for doing any work in bronze. He came to King Solomon and did all his metal work.

text.NRSV: He cast two pillars of bronze. Eighteen cubits was the height of the one, and a cord of twelve cubits would encircle it; the second pillar was the same.
text.Rotherham: And he cast two pillars of bronze,—eighteen cubits, the height of each pillar, and, a line of twelve cubits, would surround the second pillar.
text.KJV: 7:15 For he cast two pillars of brasse of eighteene cubites high a piece: and a line of twelue cubites did compasse either of them about.
text.NABRE: He fashioned two bronze columns, each eighteen cubits high and twelve cubits in circumference.

text.NRSV: He also made two capitals of molten bronze, to set on the tops of the pillars; the height of the one capital was five cubits, and the height of the other capital was five cubits.
text.Rotherham: And, two capitals, made he, to set upon the tops of the pillars, of molten bronze,—five cubits, was the height of the one capital, and, five cubits, the height of the other capital;
text.KJV: 7:16 And hee made two Chapiters of molten brasse, to set vpon the tops of the pillars: the height of the one chapiter was fiue cubites, and the height of the other chapiter was fiue cubites:
text.NABRE: He also made two capitals cast in bronze, to be placed on top of the columns, each of them five cubits high.

text.NRSV: There were nets of checkerwork with wreaths of chainwork for the capitals on the tops of the pillars; seven for the one capital, and seven for the other capital.
text.Rotherham: with a frame of checker work, wreaths of chain work, for the capitals which were upon the top of the pillars,—seven for the one capital, and seven for the other capital.
text.KJV: 7:17 And nets of checker worke, and wreathes of chaine worke, for the chapiters which were vpon the top of the pillars: seuen for the one chapiter, and seuen for the other chapiter.
text.NABRE: There were meshes made like netting and braid made like chains for the capitals on top of the columns, seven for each capital.

text.NRSV: He made the medium-sized pomegranates; there were two rows of them around each latticework to cover the capitals that were on the top of the pillars; he did the same with the other capital.
text.Rotherham: So he made the pillars,—and, two rows round about, upon the one frame of checker work, covering the capitals which were upon the top with pomegranates, thus also, did he for the other capital.
text.KJV: 7:18 And he made the pillars, and two rowes round about vpon the one networke, to couer the chapiters that were vpon the top, with pomegranates: and so did he for the other chapiter.
text.NABRE: He also cast pomegranates, two rows around each netting to cover the capital on top of the columns.

text.NRSV: Now the capitals that were on the tops of the pillars in the vestibule were of lily-work, four cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And, the capitals which were upon the top of the pillars, were of lily work, in span, four cubits.
text.KJV: 7:19 And the chapiters that were vpon the top of the pillars, were of lillie worke in the porch, foure cubites.
text.NABRE: The capitals on top of the columns (in the porch) were made like lilies, four cubits high.

text.NRSV: The capitals were on the two pillars and also above the rounded projection which was beside the latticework; there were two hundred pomegranates in rows all around on each capital.
text.Rotherham: And there were capitals upon the two pillars, above also, close by the belly, which was beside the checker work,—and there were two hundred pomegranates, in rows round about, upon either capital.
text.KJV: 7:20 And the chapiters vpon the two pillars had pomegranates also, aboue, ouer against the belly which was by the networke: and the pomegranates were two hundred in rowes round about, vpon the other chapiter.
text.NABRE: And the capitals on the two columns, both above and adjoining the bulge where it crossed out of the netting, had two hundred pomegranates in rows around each capital.

text.NRSV: He set up the pillars at the vestibule of the temple; he set up the pillar on the right and called it Jachin; and he set up the pillar on the left and called it Boaz.
text.Rotherham: So he reared the pillars for the porch of the temple,—yea he reared the right pillar, and called the name thereof Jachin, and reared the left pillar, and called the name thereof, Boaz.
text.KJV: 7:21 And he set vp the pillars in the porch of the temple: and hee set vp the right pillar, and called the name therof Iachin: and he set vp the left pillar, and called the name thereof Boaz.
text.NABRE: He set up the columns at the temple porch; one he set up to the south, and called it Jachin, and the other to the north, and called it Boaz.

text.NRSV: On the tops of the pillars was lily-work. Thus the work of the pillars was finished.
text.Rotherham: And, upon the top of the pillars, was lily work,—which gave finish to the work of the pillars.
text.KJV: 7:22 And vpon the top of the pillars was lillie worke: so was the worke of the pillars finished.
text.NABRE: The top of the columns was made like a lily. Thus the work on the columns was completed.

text.NRSV: Then he made the molten sea; it was round, ten cubits from brim to brim, and five cubits high. A line of thirty cubits would encircle it completely.
text.Rotherham: And he made a molten sea,—ten cubits from the one brim to the other, it was round all about, and, five cubits, was the height thereof, and, a line of thirty cubits, would encompass it round about.
text.KJV: 7:23 ¶ And he made a moulten Sea, ten cubites from the one brim to the other: it was round all about, & his height was fiue cubits: and a line of thirtie cubites did compasse it round about.
text.NABRE: Then he made the molten sea; it was made with a circular rim, and measured ten cubits across, five in height, and thirty in circumference.

text.NRSV: Under its brim were panels all around, ten to a cubit, surrounding the sea everywhere; the panels were in two rows, cast when it was cast.
text.Rotherham: And, colocynths, were under the brim thereof round about, encompassing it, ten in a cubit, encircling the sea, round about,—two rows of colocynths, made in the casting thereof:
text.KJV: 7:24 And vnder the brimme of it round about there were knops compassing it, ten in a cubite, compassing the sea round about: the knops were cast in two rowes, when it was cast.
text.NABRE: Under the brim, gourds encircled it for ten cubits around the compass of the sea; the gourds were in two rows and were cast in one mold with the sea.

text.NRSV: It stood on twelve oxen, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east; the sea was set on them. The hindquarters of each were toward the inside.
text.Rotherham: standing upon twelve oxen, three looking northward, and three looking westward, and three looking southward, and three looking eastward, the sea, being upon them above,—all their hinder parts, being, inward.
text.KJV: 7:25 It stood vpon twelue oxen, three looking toward the North, and three looking toward the West, and three looking toward the South, and three looking toward the East: and the Sea was set aboue vpon them, and all their hinder parts were inward.
text.NABRE: This rested on twelve oxen, three facing north, three facing west, three facing south, and three facing east, with their haunches all toward the center; upon them was set the sea.

text.NRSV: Its thickness was a handbreadth; its brim was made like the brim of a cup, like the flower of a lily; it held two thousand baths.
text.Rotherham: And, the thickness thereof, was a hand breadth, and, the brim thereof, was like the brim-work of a cup, of lily-blossoms,—two thousand baths, did it contain.
text.KJV: 7:26 And it was an hand breadth thicke, and the brimme thereof was wrought like the brim of a cup, with flowres of lillies: it contained two thousand Baths.
text.NABRE: It was a handbreadth thick, and its brim resembled that of a cup, being lily-shaped. Its capacity was two thousand baths.

text.NRSV: He also made the ten stands of bronze; each stand was four cubits long, four cubits wide, and three cubits high.
text.Rotherham: And he made ten stands of bronze,—four cubits, the length of each stand, and, four cubits, the breadth thereof, and, three cubits, the height thereof.
text.KJV: 7:27 ¶ And he made ten bases of brasse; foure cubites was the length of one base, and foure cubites the breadth thereof, and three cubites the height of it.
text.NABRE: He also made ten stands of bronze, each four cubits long, four wide, and three high.

text.NRSV: This was the construction of the stands: they had panels, and the panels were between the frames.
text.Rotherham: And, this, was the work of the stand, there were, side walls, to them,—and, the side-walls, were between joining ledges;
text.KJV: 7:28 And the worke of the bases was on this maner: they had borders, and the borders were betweene the ledges:
text.NABRE: When these stands were constructed, panels were set within the framework.

text.NRSV: On the panels that were between the frames were lions, oxen, and cherubim; on the frames, both above and below the lions and oxen, there were wreaths of beveled work.
text.Rotherham: and, upon the side-walls which were between the ledges, were lions, oxen, and cherubim, and, upon the ledges, was a pedestal above, and, beneath the lions and oxen, a wreath of hanging work.
text.KJV: 7:29 And on the borders that were betweene the ledges were lyons, oxen, and Cherubims: and vpon the ledges there was a base aboue: and beneath the lyons and oxen were certaine additions made of thinne worke.
text.NABRE: On the panels within the frames there were lions, oxen, and cherubim; and on the frames likewise, above and below the lions and oxen, there were wreaths in hammered relief.

text.NRSV: Each stand had four bronze wheels and axles of bronze; at the four corners were supports for a basin. The supports were cast with wreaths at the side of each.
text.Rotherham: And, four wheels of bronze, had each stand, with axles of bronze, and, the four steps thereof, had shoulder pieces,—under the laver, were the molten shoulder-pieces, over against each wreath.
text.KJV: 7:30 And euery base had foure brasen wheeles, and plates of brasse: and the foure corners therof had vndersetters: vnder the lauer were vndersetters molten, at the side of euery addition.
text.NABRE: Each stand had four bronze wheels and bronze axles. The four legs of each stand had cast braces, which were under the basin; they had wreaths on each side.

text.NRSV: Its opening was within a crown whose height was one cubit; its opening was round, as a stand is made, a cubit and a half deep; on its opening there were carvings; its panels were four-sided, not round.
text.Rotherham: And, the mouth thereof, within the capital and upwards, was a cubit, and, the mouth thereof, was round, of pedestal work, a cubit and a half,—moreover also, upon the mouth thereof, were gravings, with their side-walls four-square, not round.
text.KJV: 7:31 And the mouth of it within the chapiter, and aboue, was a cubite: but the mouth thereof was round after the worke of the base, a cubite and an halfe: and also vpon the mouth of it were grauings with their borders, foure square not round.
text.NABRE: The mouth of the basin was inside, and a cubit above, the crown, whose opening was round, made like a receptacle, a cubit and a half in depth. There was carved work at the opening, on panels that were square, not circular.

text.NRSV: The four wheels were underneath the panels; the axles of the wheels were in the stand; and the height of a wheel was a cubit and a half.
text.Rotherham: And, four wheels, were beneath the side-walls, the axletrees of the wheels, being in the stands,—and, the height of each wheel, was a cubit and a half:
text.KJV: 7:32 And vnder the borders were foure wheeles: & the axletrees of the wheeles were ioyned to the base, and the height of a wheele was a cubite and halfe a cubite.
text.NABRE: The four wheels were below the paneling, and the axletrees of the wheels and the stand were of one piece. Each wheel was a cubit and a half high.

text.NRSV: The wheels were made like a chariot wheel; their axles, their rims, their spokes, and their hubs were all cast.
text.Rotherham: and, the work of the wheels, was like the work of a chariot-wheel; and, their axletrees, and their felloes, and their spokes, and their naves, the whole, were molten.
text.KJV: 7:33 And the worke of the wheeles was like the worke of a charet wheele: their axletrees and their naues, and their felloes, and their spokes were all molten.
text.NABRE: The wheels were constructed like chariot wheels; their axletrees, rims, spokes, and hubs were all cast.

text.NRSV: There were four supports at the four corners of each stand; the supports were of one piece with the stands.
text.Rotherham: And there were, four shoulder-pieces, at the four corners of each stand, of the stand itself, were the shoulder-pieces thereof.
text.KJV: 7:34 And there were foure vndersetters to the foure corners of one base: and the vndersetters were of the very base it selfe.
text.NABRE: The four braces reached the four corners of each stand, and formed part of the stand.

text.NRSV: On the top of the stand there was a round band half a cubit high; on the top of the stand, its stays and its panels were of one piece with it.
text.Rotherham: And, in the top of the stand, half a cubit in height, was a circular piece round about,—and, on the top of the stand, the tenons thereof, and, the side-walls thereof, were of the same.
text.KJV: 7:35 And in the top of the base was there a round compasse of halfe a cubite high: and on the top of the base the ledges thereof, and the borders thereof were of the same.
text.NABRE: At the top of the stand there was a raised collar half a cubit high, and the handles and panels on top of the stand formed part of it.

text.NRSV: On the surfaces of its stays and on its panels he carved cherubim, lions, and palm trees, where each had space, with wreaths all around.
text.Rotherham: Then carved he, upon the plates of the tenons thereof, and upon the side-walls thereof, cherubim, lions, and palm-trees,—according to the open space of each with a wreath round about.
text.KJV: 7:36 For on the plates of the ledges thereof, and on the borders thereof, he graued Cherubims, lions, and palme trees, according to the proportion of euery one, and additions round about.
text.NABRE: On the flat ends of the handles and on the panels, wherever there was a bare space, cherubim, lions, and palm trees were carved, as well as wreaths all around.

text.NRSV: In this way he made the ten stands; all of them were cast alike, with the same measure and the same form.
text.Rotherham: Thus, made he the ten stands,—one mould, one measure, one shape, had they, all.
text.KJV: 7:37 After this maner he made the ten bases: all of them had one casting, one measure, and one size.
text.NABRE: This was how he made the ten stands, all of the same casting, the same size, the same shape.

text.NRSV: He made ten basins of bronze; each basin held forty baths, each basin measured four cubits; there was a basin on each of the ten stands.
text.Rotherham: Then made he ten lavers of bronze,—forty baths, would each laver contain, four cubits, was each laver, one laver, was on each of, the ten stands.
text.KJV: 7:38 ¶ Then made hee ten lauers of brasse: one lauer conteined fourtie baths: and euery lauer was foure cubites, and vpon euery one of the ten bases, one lauer.
text.NABRE: He made ten bronze basins, each four cubits in diameter with a capacity of forty baths, one basin atop each of the ten stands.

text.NRSV: He set five of the stands on the south side of the house, and five on the north side of the house; he set the sea on the southeast corner of the house.
text.Rotherham: And he set the stands, five on the right side of the house, and five on the left side of the house,—but, the sea, he set on the right side of the house eastward, over against the south.
text.KJV: 7:39 And he put fiue bases on the right side of the house, and fiue on the left side of the house: and he set the Sea on the right side of the house Eastward, ouer against the South.
text.NABRE: He placed the stands, five on the south side of the house and five on the north. The sea he placed off to the southeast from the south side of the house.

text.NRSV: Hiram also made the pots, the shovels, and the basins. So Hiram finished all the work that he did for King Solomon on the house of the Lord:
