text.NABRE: He arose and went to Zarephath. When he arrived at the entrance of the city, a widow was there gathering sticks; he called out to her, “Please bring me a small cupful of water to drink.”

text.NRSV: As she was going to bring it, he called to her and said, “Bring me a morsel of bread in your hand.”
text.Rotherham: And, as she went to fetch it, he called to her and said, Bring me, I pray thee, a morsel of bread, in thy hand.
text.KJV: 17:11 And as shee was going to fetch it, he called to her, and said, Bring mee, I pray thee, a morsell of bread in thine hand.
text.NABRE: She left to get it, and he called out after her, “Please bring along a crust of bread.”

text.NRSV: But she said, “As the Lord your God lives, I have nothing baked, only a handful of meal in a jar, and a little oil in a jug; I am now gathering a couple of sticks, so that I may go home and prepare it for myself and my son, that we may eat it, and die.”
text.Rotherham: And she said—By the life of Yahweh, thy God, verily I have not a cake, only a handful of meal in the jar, and a little oil in the cruse,—and lo! I have been gathering a couple of sticks, so I shall go in and make it ready for me and for my son, that we may eat it—and die!
text.KJV: 17:12 And she said, As the Lord thy God liueth, I haue not a cake, but an handfull of meale in a barrell, and a little oyle in a cruse: and behold, I am gathering two stickes, that I may goe in, and dresse it for me and my sonne, that we may eate it, and die.
text.NABRE: She said, “As the Lord, your God, lives, I have nothing baked; there is only a handful of flour in my jar and a little oil in my jug. Just now I was collecting a few sticks, to go in and prepare something for myself and my son; when we have eaten it, we shall die.”

text.NRSV: Elijah said to her, “Do not be afraid; go and do as you have said; but first make me a little cake of it and bring it to me, and afterwards make something for yourself and your son.
text.Rotherham: And Elijah said unto her—Do not fear, go in—make ready according to thy word,—howbeit, make me thereof a little cake, first, and bring it out to me, and, for thyself and thy son, make ready, afterwards.
text.KJV: 17:13 And Eliiah said vnto her, Feare not, goe, and doe as thou hast said: but make mee thereof a little cake first, and bring it vnto mee, and after make for thee, and for thy sonne.
text.NABRE: Elijah said to her, “Do not be afraid. Go and do as you have said. But first make me a little cake and bring it to me. Afterwards you can prepare something for yourself and your son.

text.NRSV: For thus says the Lord the God of Israel: The jar of meal will not be emptied and the jug of oil will not fail until the day that the Lord sends rain on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: For, thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, The jar of meal, shall not waste, and, the cruse of oil, shall not fail,—until the day that Yahweh giveth rain upon the face of the ground.
text.KJV: 17:14 For thus saith the Lord God of Israel, The barrell of meale shall not waste, neither shall the cruse of oile faile, vntill the day that the Lord sendeth raine vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: For the Lord, the God of Israel, says: The jar of flour shall not go empty, nor the jug of oil run dry, until the day when the Lord sends rain upon the earth.”

text.NRSV: She went and did as Elijah said, so that she as well as he and her household ate for many days.
text.Rotherham: So she went and made ready, according to the word of Elijah,—and did eat, she, and he, and her house, [certain] days:
text.KJV: 17:15 And shee went, and did according to the saying of Eliiah: and she, and he, and her house did eate many dayes.
text.NABRE: She left and did as Elijah had said. She had enough to eat for a long time—he and she and her household.

text.NRSV: The jar of meal was not emptied, neither did the jug of oil fail, according to the word of the Lord that he spoke by Elijah.
text.Rotherham: The jar of meal, did not waste,—and, the cruse of oil, did not fail,—according to the word of Yahweh, which he spake through Elijah.
text.KJV: 17:16 And the barrell of meale wasted not, neither did the cruse of oyle faile, according to the word of the Lord, which he spake by Eliiah.
text.NABRE: The jar of flour did not go empty, nor the jug of oil run dry, according to the word of the Lord spoken through Elijah.

text.NRSV: After this the son of the woman, the mistress of the house, became ill; his illness was so severe that there was no breath left in him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after these things, that the son of the woman, owner of the house, fell sick,—yea it came to pass that his sickness was very severe, until no, breath, was left in him.
text.KJV: 17:17 ¶ And it came to passe after these things, that the sonne of the woman, the mistresse of the house, fell sicke, and his sickenesse was so sore, that there was no breath left in him.
text.NABRE: Some time later the son of the woman, the owner of the house, fell sick, and his sickness grew more severe until he stopped breathing.

text.NRSV: She then said to Elijah, “What have you against me, O man of God? Have you come to me to bring my sin to remembrance, and to cause the death of my son?”
text.Rotherham: Then said she unto Elijah, What have I in common with thee, O man of God? Hast thou come unto me, to call to remembrance mine iniquity, and to cause the death of my son?
text.KJV: 17:18 And shee sayd vnto Eliiah, What haue I to doe with thee? O thou man of God! Art thou come vnto me to call my sinne to remembrance, and to slay my sonne?
text.NABRE: So she said to Elijah, “Why have you done this to me, man of God? Have you come to me to call attention to my guilt and to kill my son?”

text.NRSV: But he said to her, “Give me your son.” He took him from her bosom, carried him up into the upper chamber where he was lodging, and laid him on his own bed.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto her—Give me thy son. And he took him out of her bosom, and carried him unto the upper room, where, he, was staying, and laid him upon his own bed.
text.KJV: 17:19 And he said vnto her, Giue me thy sonne. And he tooke him out of her bosome, and caried him vp into a loft, where he abode, and laide him vpon his owne bed.
text.NABRE: Elijah said to her, “Give me your son.” Taking him from her lap, he carried him to the upper room where he was staying, and laid him on his own bed.

text.NRSV: He cried out to the Lord, “O Lord my God, have you brought calamity even upon the widow with whom I am staying, by causing the death of her son?”
text.Rotherham: Then cried he unto Yahweh, and said,—O Yahweh, my God! Even upon the widow with whom I am sojourning, hast thou brought misfortune by causing the death of her son?
text.KJV: 17:20 And hee cried vnto the Lord, and said, O Lord my God, hast thou also brought euill vpon the widow, with whom I soiourne, by slaying her sonne?
text.NABRE: He called out to the Lord: “ Lord, my God, will you afflict even the widow with whom I am staying by killing her son?”

text.NRSV: Then he stretched himself upon the child three times, and cried out to the Lord, “O Lord my God, let this child’s life come into him again.”
text.Rotherham: And he stretched himself out upon the boy, three times, and cried unto Yahweh, and said,—O Yahweh, my God! Let the life of this boy, I pray thee, come again within him.
text.KJV: 17:21 And he stretched himselfe vpon the child three times, and cried vnto the Lord, and said; O Lord my God, I pray thee, let this childes soule come into him againe.
text.NABRE: Then he stretched himself out upon the child three times and he called out to the Lord: “ Lord, my God, let the life breath return to the body of this child.”

text.NRSV: The Lord listened to the voice of Elijah; the life of the child came into him again, and he revived.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh hearkened unto the voice of Elijah,—and the life of the boy came again within him, and he lived.
text.KJV: 17:22 And the Lord heard the voice of Eliiah, and the soule of the child came into him againe, and he reuiued.
text.NABRE: The Lord heard the prayer of Elijah; the life breath returned to the child’s body and he lived.

text.NRSV: Elijah took the child, brought him down from the upper chamber into the house, and gave him to his mother; then Elijah said, “See, your son is alive.”
text.Rotherham: Then Elijah took the boy, and carried him down out of the upper room, into the house, and gave him to his mother,—and Elijah said, See! thy son liveth.
text.KJV: 17:23 And Eliiah tooke the childe, and brought him downe out of the chamber into the house, and deliuered him vnto his mother: and Eliiah said, See, thy sonne liueth.
text.NABRE: Taking the child, Elijah carried him down into the house from the upper room and gave him to his mother. Elijah said, “See! Your son is alive.”

text.NRSV: So the woman said to Elijah, “Now I know that you are a man of God, and that the word of the Lord in your mouth is truth.”
text.Rotherham: And the woman said unto Elijah, Now, then I know, that, a man of God, thou art,—and that, the word of Yahweh, is in thy mouth, of a truth.
text.KJV: 17:24 ¶ And the woman said to Eliiah, Now by this I know, that thou art a man of God, and that the word of the Lord in thy mouth is trueth.
text.NABRE: The woman said to Elijah, “Now indeed I know that you are a man of God, and it is truly the word of the Lord that you speak.”

text.NRSV: After many days the word of the Lord came to Elijah, in the third year of the drought, saying, “Go, present yourself to Ahab; I will send rain on the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after many days, that, the word of Yahweh, came unto Elijah, in the third year, saying,—Go, shew thyself unto Ahab, that I may send rain upon the face of the ground.
text.KJV: 18:1 And it came to passe after many daies, that the word of the Lord came to Eliiah in the third yeere, saying, Goe shewe thy selfe vnto Ahab, and I will send raine vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: Chapter 18 Elijah Ends the Drought. Long afterward, in the third year, the word of the Lord came to Elijah: Go, present yourself to Ahab, that I may send rain upon the earth.

text.NRSV: So Elijah went to present himself to Ahab. Now the famine was severe in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: And Elijah went, to show himself unto Ahab. Now, the famine, was severe in Samaria.
text.KJV: 18:2 And Eliiah went to shew himselfe vnto Ahab, and there was a sore famine in Samaria.
text.NABRE: So Elijah went to present himself to Ahab. Now the famine in Samaria was severe,

text.NRSV: Ahab summoned Obadiah, who was in charge of the palace. (Now Obadiah revered the Lord greatly;
text.Rotherham: And Ahab had called for Obadiah, who was over his house. Now, Obadiah, was one who revered Yahweh, exceedingly.
text.KJV: 18:3 And Ahab called Obadiah which was the gouernour of his house: (now Obadiah feared the Lord greatly:
text.NABRE: and Ahab had summoned Obadiah, master of his palace, who greatly revered the Lord.

text.NRSV: when Jezebel was killing off the prophets of the Lord, Obadiah took a hundred prophets, hid them fifty to a cave, and provided them with bread and water.)
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass, when Jezebel was cutting off the prophets of Yahweh, that Obadiah took a hundred prophets, and hid them by fifties in caves, and sustained them, with bread and water.
text.KJV: 18:4 For it was so, when Iezebel cut off the Prophets of the Lord, that Obadiah tooke an hundred Prophets, and hid them by fiftie in a caue, and fed them with bread and water.)
text.NABRE: When Jezebel was slaughtering the prophets of the Lord, Obadiah took a hundred prophets, hid them away by fifties in caves, and supplied them with food and water.

text.NRSV: Then Ahab said to Obadiah, “Go through the land to all the springs of water and to all the wadis; perhaps we may find grass to keep the horses and mules alive, and not lose some of the animals.”
text.Rotherham: So then Ahab said unto Obadiah, Go, and let us pass throughout the land, unto all the fountains of water, and unto all the ravines, peradventure we may find grass, and save alive horse and mule, and not have more of the beasts cut off.
text.KJV: 18:5 And Ahab said vnto Obadiah, Goe into the land, vnto all fountaines of water, and vnto all brookes: peraduenture we may finde grasse to saue the horses and mules aliue, that we leese not all the beasts.
text.NABRE: Ahab said to Obadiah, “Go through the land to all sources of water and to all the wadies. We may find grass and keep the horses and mules alive, so that we shall not have to slaughter any of the beasts.”

text.NRSV: So they divided the land between them to pass through it; Ahab went one way by himself, and Obadiah went another way by himself.
text.Rotherham: So they divided to them the land, to pass through it,—Ahab, went one way, by himself, and, Obadiah, went another way, by himself.
text.KJV: 18:6 So they diuided the land betweene them to passe throughout it: Ahab went one way by himselfe, and Obadiah went another way by himselfe.
text.NABRE: Dividing the land to explore between them, Ahab went one way by himself, Obadiah another way by himself.

text.NRSV: As Obadiah was on the way, Elijah met him; Obadiah recognized him, fell on his face, and said, “Is it you, my lord Elijah?”
text.Rotherham: And so it was, as Obadiah was on the road, that lo! Elijah, met him,—and he recognised him, and fell upon his face, and said—Now art, thou, my lord Elijah?
text.KJV: 18:7 ¶ And as Obadiah was in the way, behold, Eliiah met him: and hee knew him, and fell on his face, and said; Art thou that my lord Eliiah?
text.NABRE: As Obadiah was on his way, Elijah met him. Recognizing him, Obadiah fell prostrate and asked, “Is it you, my lord Elijah?”

text.NRSV: He answered him, “It is I. Go, tell your lord, ‘Elijah is here.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said to him—I, am! Go, say to thy lord: Here, is, Elijah!
text.KJV: 18:8 And he answered him, I am: goe, tell thy lord, Behold, Eliiah is here.
text.NABRE: He said to him, “Yes. Go tell your lord, ‘Elijah is here!’”

text.NRSV: And he said, “How have I sinned, that you would hand your servant over to Ahab, to kill me?
text.Rotherham: And he said—How have I sinned,—that, thou, art delivering thy servant into the hand of Ahab, to put me to death?
text.KJV: 18:9 And he said, What haue I sinned, that thou wouldest deliuer thy seruant into the hand of Ahab, to slay mee?
text.NABRE: But Obadiah said, “What sin has your servant committed, that you are handing me over to Ahab to be killed?

text.NRSV: As the Lord your God lives, there is no nation or kingdom to which my lord has not sent to seek you; and when they would say, ‘He is not here,’ he would require an oath of the kingdom or nation, that they had not found you.
text.Rotherham: By the life of Yahweh thy God, there is no nation or kingdom, whither my lord hath not sent to seek thee, and, when they have said—Nay! he hath taken an oath of the kingdom or the nation, that no one could find thee;
text.KJV: 18:10 As the Lord thy God liueth, there is no nation or kingdome, whither my lord hath not sent to seeke thee: and when they said, He is not there, hee tooke an oath of the kingdome and nation, that they found thee not.
text.NABRE: As the Lord, your God, lives, there is no nation or kingdom where my lord has not sent in search of you. When they replied, ‘He is not here,’ he made each kingdom and nation swear they could not find you.

text.NRSV: But now you say, ‘Go, tell your lord, “Elijah is here.”’
text.Rotherham: and, now, thou, art saying,—Go say to thy lord, Here, is, Elijah;
text.KJV: 18:11 And now thou sayest, Goe, tell thy lord, Behold, Eliiah is here.
text.NABRE: And now you say, ‘Go tell your lord: Elijah is here!’

text.NRSV: As soon as I have gone from you, the spirit of the Lord will carry you I know not where; so, when I come and tell Ahab and he cannot find you, he will kill me, although I your servant have revered the Lord from my youth.
text.Rotherham: so shall it come to pass, as soon as, I, go from thee, that, the spirit of Yahweh, shall carry thee away—whither I know not, and so, when I go in to tell Ahab, and he cannot find thee, then will he slay me,—and yet, thy servant, hath revered Yahweh, from my youth.
text.KJV: 18:12 And it shall come to passe, as soone as I am gone from thee, that the spirit of the Lord shall cary thee whither I know not; and so when I come and tell Ahab, and he cannot finde thee, he shall slay mee: but I thy seruant feare the Lord from my youth.
text.NABRE: After I leave you, the spirit of the Lord will carry you to some place I do not know, and when I go to inform Ahab and he does not find you, he will kill me—though your servant has revered the Lord from his youth!

text.NRSV: Has it not been told my lord what I did when Jezebel killed the prophets of the Lord, how I hid a hundred of the Lord’s prophets fifty to a cave, and provided them with bread and water?
text.Rotherham: Was it not told my lord, what I did when Jezebel was slaying the prophets of Yahweh,—how I hid, of the prophets of Yahweh, a hundred men—by fifties—in caves, and sustained them with bread and water?
text.KJV: 18:13 Was it not told my lord, what I did when Iezebel slew the Prophets of the Lord ? How I hid an hundred men of the Lords Prophets, by fiftie in a caue, and fedde them with bread and water?
text.NABRE: Have you not been told, my lord, what I did when Jezebel was murdering the prophets of the Lord —that I hid a hundred of the prophets of the Lord, fifty each in caves, and supplied them with food and water?

text.NRSV: Yet now you say, ‘Go, tell your lord, “Elijah is here”’; he will surely kill me.”
text.Rotherham: and yet, now, thou, art saying, Go, tell thy lord—Here, is, Elijah; and so he will slay me!
text.KJV: 18:14 And now thou sayest, Goe, tell thy lord, Behold, Eliiah is here: and hee shall slay me.
text.NABRE: And now you say, ‘Go tell your lord: Elijah is here!’ He will kill me!”

text.NRSV: Elijah said, “As the Lord of hosts lives, before whom I stand, I will surely present myself to him today.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah—By the life of Yahweh of hosts, before whom I stand, to-day, will I show myself unto him.
text.KJV: 18:15 And Eliiah said, As the Lord of hostes liueth, before whom I stand, I will surely shew my selfe vnto him to day.
text.NABRE: Elijah answered, “As the Lord of hosts lives, whom I serve, I will present myself to him today.”

text.NRSV: So Obadiah went to meet Ahab, and told him; and Ahab went to meet Elijah.
text.Rotherham: So Obadiah went to meet Ahab, and told him,—and, Ahab, went to meet, Elijah.
text.KJV: 18:16 So Obadiah went to meete Ahab, and told him: and Ahab went to meete Eliiah.
text.NABRE: So Obadiah went to meet Ahab and informed him, and Ahab came to meet Elijah.

text.NRSV: When Ahab saw Elijah, Ahab said to him, “Is it you, you troubler of Israel?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Ahab saw Elijah, that Ahab said unto him, Now, art, thou, troubling Israel?
text.KJV: 18:17 ¶ And it came to passe when Ahab saw Eliiah, that Ahab saide vnto him, Art thou hee that troubleth Israel?
text.NABRE: When Ahab saw Elijah, he said to him, “Is it you, you disturber of Israel?”

text.NRSV: He answered, “I have not troubled Israel; but you have, and your father’s house, because you have forsaken the commandments of the Lord and followed the Baals.
text.Rotherham: And he said—I have not troubled Israel, but, thou and the house of thy father,—in that ye have forsaken the commandments of Yahweh, and followed Baal.
text.KJV: 18:18 And hee answered, I haue not troubled Israel, but thou and thy fathers house, in that yee haue forsaken the Commandements of the Lord, and thou hast followed Baalim.
text.NABRE: He answered, “It is not I who disturb Israel, but you and your father’s house, by forsaking the commands of the Lord and you by following the Baals.

text.NRSV: Now therefore summon all Israel to me at Mount Carmel, with the four hundred fifty prophets of Baal and the four hundred prophets of Asherah, who eat at Jezebel’s table.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, send, gather unto me all Israel, unto Mount Carmel,—and the prophets of Baal, four hundred and fifty, and the prophets of the Sacred Stem, four hundred, who do eat at the table of Jezebel.
text.KJV: 18:19 Now therefore send, and gather to mee all Israel vnto mount Carmel, and the prophets of Baal foure hundred and fiftie, and the prophets of the groues foure hundred, which eate at Iezebels table.
text.NABRE: Now summon all Israel to me on Mount Carmel, as well as the four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal and the four hundred prophets of Asherah who eat at Jezebel’s table.”

text.NRSV: So Ahab sent to all the Israelites, and assembled the prophets at Mount Carmel.
text.Rotherham: So Ahab sent among all the sons of Israel,—and gathered the prophets unto Mount Carmel.
text.KJV: 18:20 So Ahab sent vnto all the children of Israel, and gathered the prophets together vnto mount Carmel.
text.NABRE: So Ahab summoned all the Israelites and had the prophets gather on Mount Carmel.

text.NRSV: Elijah then came near to all the people, and said, “How long will you go limping with two different opinions? If the Lord is God, follow him; but if Baal, then follow him.” The people did not answer him a word.
text.Rotherham: And Elijah drew near unto all the people, and said—How long are ye limping on the two divided opinions? If, Yahweh, be GOD, follow, him, but, if, Baal, follow, him. But the people answered him, not a word.
text.KJV: 18:21 And Eliiah came vnto all the people, and said, how long halt yee betweene two opinions? If the Lord bee God, follow him: but if Baal, then follow him: and the people answered him not a word.
text.NABRE: Elijah approached all the people and said, “How long will you straddle the issue? If the Lord is God, follow him; if Baal, follow him.” But the people did not answer him.

text.NRSV: Then Elijah said to the people, “I, even I only, am left a prophet of the Lord; but Baal’s prophets are four hundred fifty.
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah unto the people—I, am left, Yahweh’s, only, prophet,—but, the prophets of Baal, are four hundred and fifty men.
text.KJV: 18:22 Then said Eliiah vnto the people, I, euen I onely remaine a Prophet of the Lord: but Baals prophets are foure hundred and fiftie men.
text.NABRE: So Elijah said to the people, “I am the only remaining prophet of the Lord, and there are four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal.

text.NRSV: Let two bulls be given to us; let them choose one bull for themselves, cut it in pieces, and lay it on the wood, but put no fire to it; I will prepare the other bull and lay it on the wood, but put no fire to it.
text.Rotherham: Let there be given us, therefore, two bullocks, and let them choose for themselves one bullock, and cut it in pieces, and lay it upon the wood, but, fire, shall they not put,—then, I, will make ready the other bullock, and place upon the wood, but, fire, will I not put.
text.KJV: 18:23 Let them therefore giue vs two bullocks, and let them chuse one bullocke for themselues, and cut it in pieces, and lay it on wood, and put no fire vnder: and I will dresse the other bullocke, and lay it on wood, and put no fire vnder.
text.NABRE: Give us two young bulls. Let them choose one, cut it into pieces, and place it on the wood, but start no fire. I shall prepare the other and place it on the wood, but shall start no fire.

text.NRSV: Then you call on the name of your god and I will call on the name of the Lord; the god who answers by fire is indeed God.” All the people answered, “Well spoken!”
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye call on the name of your god, and, I, will call on the name of Yahweh, and it shall be, the God that respondeth by fire, he, is GOD. And all the people responded—Well spoken!
text.KJV: 18:24 And call ye on the name of your gods, and I will call on the Name of the Lord: and the God that answereth by fire, let him be God. And all the people answered, and said, It is well spoken.
text.NABRE: You shall call upon the name of your gods, and I will call upon the name of the Lord. The God who answers with fire is God.” All the people answered, “We agree!”

text.NRSV: Then Elijah said to the prophets of Baal, “Choose for yourselves one bull and prepare it first, for you are many; then call on the name of your god, but put no fire to it.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah to the prophets of Baal—Choose for yourselves one bullock, and make ye ready, first, for, ye, are many,—and call ye on the name of your god, but, fire, shall ye not put.
text.KJV: 18:25 And Eliiah said vnto the prophets of Baal, Chuse you one bullocke for your selues, and dresse it first, for yee are many: and call on the name of your gods, but put no fire vnder.
text.NABRE: Elijah then said to the prophets of Baal, “Choose one young bull and prepare it first, for there are more of you. Call upon your gods, but do not start the fire.”

text.NRSV: So they took the bull that was given them, prepared it, and called on the name of Baal from morning until noon, saying, “O Baal, answer us!” But there was no voice, and no answer. They limped about the altar that they had made.
text.Rotherham: So they took the bullock which was given them, and made ready, and called on the name of Baal—from the morning even until the noon, saying—O Baal! answer us. But them was no voice, nor any that answered. And they leaped about by the altar which had been made.
text.KJV: 18:26 And they took the bullocke which was giuen them, and they dressed it, and called on the name of Baal from morning, euen vntil noone, saying, O Baal, heare vs. But there was no voyce, nor any that answered: And they leapt vpon the altar which was made.
text.NABRE: Taking the young bull that was turned over to them, they prepared it and called upon Baal from morning to noon, saying, “Baal, answer us!” But there was no sound, and no one answering. And they hopped around the altar they had prepared.

text.NRSV: At noon Elijah mocked them, saying, “Cry aloud! Surely he is a god; either he is meditating, or he has wandered away, or he is on a journey, or perhaps he is asleep and must be awakened.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at noon, that Elijah mocked them, and said—Cry with a loud voice, for, a god, he is, either he hath, a meditation, or an occasion to retire, or he hath, a journey,—peradventure, he, sleepeth, and must be awaked.
text.KJV: 18:27 And it came to passe at noone, that Eliiah mocked them, and saide, Crie aloud: for he is a god, either he is talking, or he is pursuing, or hee is in a iourney, or peraduenture he sleepeth, and must be awaked.
text.NABRE: When it was noon, Elijah taunted them: “Call louder, for he is a god; he may be busy doing his business, or may be on a journey. Perhaps he is asleep and must be awakened.”

text.NRSV: Then they cried aloud, and, as was their custom, they cut themselves with swords and lances until the blood gushed out over them.
text.Rotherham: And they cried, with a loud voice, and cut themselves, after their custom, with swords, and with lances,—until the blood gushed out upon them.
text.KJV: 18:28 And they cried loud, and cut themselues after their maner, with kniues, and lancers, till the blood gushed out vpon them.
text.NABRE: They called out louder and slashed themselves with swords and spears according to their ritual until blood gushed over them.

text.NRSV: As midday passed, they raved on until the time of the offering of the oblation, but there was no voice, no answer, and no response.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when noon was passed, that although they prophesied until the offering up of the evening gift, yet was there no voice nor any that answered, nor any that hearkened.
text.KJV: 18:29 And it came to passe when midday was past, and they prophesied vntil the time of the offering of the euening sacrifice; that there was neither voice, nor any to answere, nor any that regarded.
text.NABRE: Noon passed and they remained in a prophetic state until the time for offering sacrifice. But there was no sound, no one answering, no one listening.

text.NRSV: Then Elijah said to all the people, “Come closer to me”; and all the people came closer to him. First he repaired the altar of the Lord that had been thrown down;
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah unto all the people: Draw near unto, me. And all the people drew near unto, him. Then repaired he the broken-down altar of Yahweh; yea Elijah took twelve stones, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Jacob,—
text.KJV: 18:30 And Eliiah said vnto all the people, Come neere vnto me. And all the people came neere vnto him. And he repaired the Altar of the Lord that was broken downe.
text.NABRE: Then Elijah said to all the people, “Come here to me.” When they drew near to him, he repaired the altar of the Lord which had been destroyed.

text.NRSV: Elijah took twelve stones, according to the number of the tribes of the sons of Jacob, to whom the word of the Lord came, saying, “Israel shall be your name”;
text.Rotherham: unto whom the word of Yahweh came, saying—Israel, shall be thy name;
text.KJV: 18:31 And Eliiah tooke twelue stones, according to the number of the tribes of the sonnes of Iacob, vnto whome the word of the Lord came, saying, Israel shall bee thy name.
text.NABRE: He took twelve stones, for the number of tribes of the sons of Jacob, to whom the Lord had said: Israel shall be your name.

text.NRSV: with the stones he built an altar in the name of the Lord. Then he made a trench around the altar, large enough to contain two measures of seed.
text.Rotherham: and built, with the stones, an altar, in the name of Yahweh,—and he made a trench, as large as would contain two measures of seed, round about the altar;
text.KJV: 18:32 And with the stones hee built an altar in the Name of the Lord, and hee made a trench about the altar, as great as would containe two measures of seed.
text.NABRE: He built the stones into an altar to the name of the Lord, and made a trench around the altar large enough for two measures of grain.

text.NRSV: Next he put the wood in order, cut the bull in pieces, and laid it on the wood.
text.Rotherham: and he put in order the wood,—and cut in pieces the bullock, and laid upon the wood. Then said he—Fill four pitchers with water, and pour out upon the ascending-sacrifice, and upon the wood. And they did so.
text.KJV: 18:33 And he put the wood in order, and cut the bullocke in pieces, and laide him on the wood, and said, Fill foure barrels with water, and powre it on the burnt sacrifice, and on the wood.
text.NABRE: When he had arranged the wood, he cut up the young bull and laid it on the wood.

text.NRSV: He said, “Fill four jars with water and pour it on the burnt offering and on the wood.” Then he said, “Do it a second time”; and they did it a second time. Then he said, “Do it a third time”; and they did it a third time,
text.Rotherham: And he said—Do it the second time. And they did it the second time. And he said—Do it the third time. And they did it the third time.
text.KJV: 18:34 And hee said, Doe it the second time. And they did it the second time. And he said, Doe it the third time. And they did it the third time.
text.NABRE: He said, “Fill four jars with water and pour it over the burnt offering and over the wood.” “Do it again,” he said, and they did it again. “Do it a third time,” he said, and they did it a third time.

text.NRSV: so that the water ran all around the altar, and filled the trench also with water.
text.Rotherham: And the water ran round about the altar,—moreover also, the trench, filled he with water.
text.KJV: 18:35 And the water ran round about the altar, and hee filled the trench also with water.
text.NABRE: The water flowed around the altar; even the trench was filled with the water.

text.NRSV: At the time of the offering of the oblation, the prophet Elijah came near and said, “O Lord, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, let it be known this day that you are God in Israel, that I am your servant, and that I have done all these things at your bidding.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the time of the offering of the gift, that Elijah the prophet drew near, and said, O Yahweh, God of Abraham, Isaac and Israel! To-day, let it be known—That, thou, art God in Israel, and that, I, am thy servant,—and that, by thy word, have I done all these things.
text.KJV: 18:36 And it came to passe at the time of the offering of the euening sacrifice, that Eliiah the prophet came neere and said, Lord God of Abraham, Isaac, and of Israel, Let it bee knowen this day that thou art God in Israel, and that I am thy seruant, and that I haue done all these things at thy word.
text.NABRE: At the time for offering sacrifice, Elijah the prophet came forward and said, “ Lord, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, let it be known this day that you are God in Israel and that I am your servant and have done all these things at your command.

text.NRSV: Answer me, O Lord, answer me, so that this people may know that you, O Lord, are God, and that you have turned their hearts back.”
text.Rotherham: Answer me, O Yahweh! answer me, That this people may know, that, thou—Yahweh, art GOD,—so shalt, thou thyself, have turned their heart back again.
text.KJV: 18:37 Heare me, O Lord, heare me, that this people may know that thou art the Lord God, and that thou hast turned their heart backe againe.
text.NABRE: Answer me, Lord! Answer me, that this people may know that you, Lord, are God and that you have turned their hearts back to you.”

text.NRSV: Then the fire of the Lord fell and consumed the burnt offering, the wood, the stones, and the dust, and even licked up the water that was in the trench.
text.Rotherham: Then fell fire from Yahweh, and consumed the ascending-sacrifice, and the wood, and the stones, and the dust,—even the water that was in the trench, it licked up.
text.KJV: 18:38 Then the fire of the Lord fell, and consumed the burnt sacrifice, and the wood, and the stones, and the dust, and licked vp the water that was in the trench.
text.NABRE: The Lord ’s fire came down and devoured the burnt offering, wood, stones, and dust, and lapped up the water in the trench.

text.NRSV: When all the people saw it, they fell on their faces and said, “The Lord indeed is God; the Lord indeed is God.”
text.Rotherham: And, when all the people saw it, they fell upon their faces,—and said, Yahweh, he, is GOD, Yahweh, he, is GOD.
text.KJV: 18:39 And when all the people sawe it, they fell on their faces: and they saide, The Lord, he is the God, the Lord, he is the God.
text.NABRE: Seeing this, all the people fell prostrate and said, “The Lord is God! The Lord is God!”

text.NRSV: Elijah said to them, “Seize the prophets of Baal; do not let one of them escape.” Then they seized them; and Elijah brought them down to the Wadi Kishon, and killed them there.
text.Rotherham: And Elijah said unto them—Seize ye the prophets of Baal—let not, a man, of them escape. So they seized them. And Elijah took them down unto the ravine of Kishon, and slaughtered them there.
text.KJV: 18:40 And Eliiah saide vnto them, Take the prophets of Baal, let not one of them escape: And they tooke them, and Eliiah brought them downe to the brooke Kishon, and slewe them there.
text.NABRE: Then Elijah said to them, “Seize the prophets of Baal. Let none of them escape!” They seized them, and Elijah brought them down to the Wadi Kishon and there he slaughtered them.

text.NRSV: Elijah said to Ahab, “Go up, eat and drink; for there is a sound of rushing rain.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Elijah unto Ahab, Go up, eat and drink,—for there is a sound of a downpour of rain.
text.KJV: 18:41 ¶ And Eliiah said vnto Ahab, Get thee vp, eate and drinke, for there is a sound of abundance of raine.
text.NABRE: Elijah then said to Ahab, “Go up, eat and drink, for there is the sound of a heavy rain.”

text.NRSV: So Ahab went up to eat and to drink. Elijah went up to the top of Carmel; there he bowed himself down upon the earth and put his face between his knees.
text.Rotherham: So Ahab went up, to eat and to drink,—but, Elijah, went up to the top of Carmel, and bowed himself to the earth, and put his face between his knees.
text.KJV: 18:42 So Ahab went vp to eate and to drinke, and Eliiah went vp to the top of Carmel, and he cast himselfe downe vpon the earth, and put his face betweene his knees,
text.NABRE: So Ahab went up to eat and drink, while Elijah went up to the top of Carmel, crouched down to the earth, and put his head between his knees.

text.NRSV: He said to his servant, “Go up now, look toward the sea.” He went up and looked, and said, “There is nothing.” Then he said, “Go again seven times.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto his young man—Go up, I pray thee, and look about in the direction of the sea. So he went up, and looked about, and said—There is, nothing whatever. Then said he—Go again, seven times.
text.KJV: 18:43 And said to his seruant, Goe vp now, looke toward the Sea. And hee went vp, and looked, and saide, There is nothing. And he said, Goe againe seuen times.
text.NABRE: He said to his servant, “Go up and look out to sea.” He went up and looked, but reported, “There is nothing.” Seven times he said, “Go look again!”

text.NRSV: At the seventh time he said, “Look, a little cloud no bigger than a person’s hand is rising out of the sea.” Then he said, “Go say to Ahab, ‘Harness your chariot and go down before the rain stops you.’”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the seventh time, that he said, Lo! a little cloud, like a man’s hand coming up from the sea. Then he said—Go up, say unto Ahab—Harness, and get thee down, lest the rain shut thee in.
text.KJV: 18:44 And it came to passe at the seuenth time, that he said, Behold, there ariseth a little cloud out of the Sea, like a mans hand. And he said, Goe vp, say vnto Ahab, Prepare thy charet, and get thee downe, that the raine stop thee not.
text.NABRE: And the seventh time the youth reported, “There is a cloud as small as a man’s hand rising from the sea.” Elijah said, “Go and say to Ahab, ‘Harness up and go down the mountain before the rain stops you.’”

text.NRSV: In a little while the heavens grew black with clouds and wind; there was a heavy rain. Ahab rode off and went to Jezreel.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, meanwhile, that, the heavens, had enshrouded themselves with clouds and wind, and there came a great rain, and Ahab rode, and went to Jezreel;
text.KJV: 18:45 And it came to passe in the meane while, that the heauen was blacke with cloudes and winde, and there was a great raine: and Ahab rode and went to Iezreel.
text.NABRE: All at once the sky grew dark with clouds and wind, and a heavy rain fell. Ahab mounted his chariot and headed for Jezreel.

text.NRSV: But the hand of the Lord was on Elijah; he girded up his loins and ran in front of Ahab to the entrance of Jezreel.
text.Rotherham: but, the hand of Yahweh, came upon Elijah, so he girded his loins,—and ran before Ahab, until thou enterest Jezreel.
text.KJV: 18:46 And the hand of the Lord was on Eliiah; and hee girded vp his loynes, and ranne before Ahab, to the entrance of Iezreel.
text.NABRE: But the hand of the Lord was on Elijah. He girded up his clothing and ran before Ahab as far as the approaches to Jezreel.

text.NRSV: Ahab told Jezebel all that Elijah had done, and how he had killed all the prophets with the sword.
text.Rotherham: And Ahab told Jezebel, all that Elijah had done,—and withal, how he had slain all the prophets with the sword.
text.KJV: 19:1 And Ahab told Iezebel all that Eliiah had done, and withall, how hee had slaine all the Prophets with the sword.
text.NABRE: Chapter 19 Flight to Horeb. Ahab told Jezebel all that Elijah had done—that he had murdered all the prophets by the sword.

text.NRSV: Then Jezebel sent a messenger to Elijah, saying, “So may the gods do to me, and more also, if I do not make your life like the life of one of them by this time tomorrow.”
text.Rotherham: Then sent Jezebel a messenger unto Elijah, saying,—So, let the gods do, and, so, let them add, if, by this time to-morrow, I make not thy life as the life of one of them.
text.KJV: 19:2 Then Iezebel sent a messenger vnto Eliiah, saying; So let the gods do to me, and more also, if I make not thy life as the life of one of them, by to morrow about this time.
text.NABRE: Jezebel then sent a messenger to Elijah and said, “May the gods do thus to me and more, if by this time tomorrow I have not done with your life what was done to each of them.”

text.NRSV: Then he was afraid; he got up and fled for his life, and came to Beer-sheba, which belongs to Judah; he left his servant there.
text.Rotherham: And, when he saw [that], he arose, and went for his life, and came to Beer-sheba, which belongeth to Judah,—and left his servant there.
text.KJV: 19:3 And when he saw that, hee arose, and went for his life, and came to Beersheba, which belongeth to Iudah, and left his seruant there.
text.NABRE: Elijah was afraid and fled for his life, going to Beer-sheba of Judah. He left his servant there

text.NRSV: But he himself went a day’s journey into the wilderness, and came and sat down under a solitary broom tree. He asked that he might die: “It is enough; now, O Lord, take away my life, for I am no better than my ancestors.”
text.Rotherham: But, he himself, went into the wilderness a day’s journey, and came and sat down under a certain shrub,—and asked for his life, that he might die, and said—Enough, now, O Yahweh! take away my life, for, no better, am, I, than, my fathers.
text.KJV: 19:4 ¶ But he himselfe went a dayes iourney into the wildernesse, and came and sate downe vnder a Iuniper tree: and hee requested for himselfe that hee might die, and sayd, It is enough, now O Lord, take away my life: for I am not better then my fathers.
text.NABRE: and went a day’s journey into the wilderness, until he came to a solitary broom tree and sat beneath it. He prayed for death: “Enough, Lord! Take my life, for I am no better than my ancestors.”

text.NRSV: He lay down under the broom tree and fell asleep. Suddenly an angel touched him and said to him, “Get up and eat.”
text.Rotherham: And, as he lay, and slept, under a certain shrub, lo! a messenger, touching him, who said to him—Rise, eat.
text.KJV: 19:5 And as hee lay and slept vnder a Iuniper tree, behold then, an Angel touched him, and sayd vnto him, Arise, and eate.
text.NABRE: He lay down and fell asleep under the solitary broom tree, but suddenly a messenger touched him and said, “Get up and eat!”

text.NRSV: He looked, and there at his head was a cake baked on hot stones, and a jar of water. He ate and drank, and lay down again.
text.Rotherham: So he looked about, and lo! at his head, a cake baked on hot stones, and a cruse of water,—and he did eat and drink, and then went back and lay down.
text.KJV: 19:6 And he looked, and behold, there was a cake baken on the coales, and a cruse of water at his head: and hee did eate and drinke, and laide him downe againe.
text.NABRE: He looked and there at his head was a hearth cake and a jug of water. After he ate and drank, he lay down again,

text.NRSV: The angel of the Lord came a second time, touched him, and said, “Get up and eat, otherwise the journey will be too much for you.”
text.Rotherham: And the messenger of Yahweh came again a second time, and touched him, and said—Rise, eat,—because, too much for thee, is the journey.
text.KJV: 19:7 And the Angel of the Lord came againe the second time, and touched him, and sayd, Arise, and eate, because the iourney is too great for thee.
text.NABRE: but the angel of the Lord came back a second time, touched him, and said, “Get up and eat or the journey will be too much for you!”

text.NRSV: He got up, and ate and drank; then he went in the strength of that food forty days and forty nights to Horeb the mount of God.
text.Rotherham: So he arose, and did eat and drink,—and journeyed, in the strength of that eating, forty days and forty nights, as far as the mountain of God—Horeb.
text.KJV: 19:8 And hee arose, and did eate and drinke, and went in the strength of that meate fourtie dayes and fourtie nights, vnto Horeb the mount of God.
text.NABRE: He got up, ate, and drank; then strengthened by that food, he walked forty days and forty nights to the mountain of God, Horeb.

text.NRSV: At that place he came to a cave, and spent the night there. Then the word of the Lord came to him, saying, “What are you doing here, Elijah?”
text.Rotherham: And he entered, there, into a cave, and lodged there,—and lo! the word of Yahweh [came] unto him, and said to him, What doest thou here, Elijah?
text.KJV: 19:9 ¶ And he came thither vnto a caue, and lodged there, and behold, the word of the Lord came to him, and he said vnto him, What doest thou here, Eliiah?
text.NABRE: There he came to a cave, where he took shelter. But the word of the Lord came to him: Why are you here, Elijah?

text.NRSV: He answered, “I have been very zealous for the Lord, the God of hosts; for the Israelites have forsaken your covenant, thrown down your altars, and killed your prophets with the sword. I alone am left, and they are seeking my life, to take it away.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Very jealous, have I been, for Yahweh, God of hosts, because the sons of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thine altars, have they thrown down, thy prophets, also have they slain with the sword,—and, I alone, am left, and they have sought my life, to take it.
text.KJV: 19:10 And hee sayd, I haue beene very iealous for the Lord God of hostes: for the children of Israel haue forsaken thy Couenant, throwen downe thine Altars, and slaine thy Prophets with the sword: and I, euen I onely am left, and they seeke my life, to take it away.
text.NABRE: He answered: “I have been most zealous for the Lord, the God of hosts, but the Israelites have forsaken your covenant. They have destroyed your altars and murdered your prophets by the sword. I alone remain, and they seek to take my life.”

text.NRSV: He said, “Go out and stand on the mountain before the Lord, for the Lord is about to pass by.” Now there was a great wind, so strong that it was splitting mountains and breaking rocks in pieces before the Lord, but the Lord was not in the wind; and after the wind an earthquake, but the Lord was not in the earthquake;
text.Rotherham: And he said—Go forth to-morrow, and stand in the mountain, before Yahweh. And lo! Yahweh, passing by, and a great and strong wind—rending the mountains, and breaking in pieces the crags, before Yahweh, Not, in the wind, was, Yahweh,—And, after the wind, an, earthquake, Not, in the earthquake, was, Yahweh;
text.KJV: 19:11 And he sayd, Goe forth, and stand vpon the mount before the Lord. And beholde, the Lord passed by, and a great and strong winde rent the mountaines, and brake in pieces the rockes, before the Lord; but the Lord was not in the winde: and after the winde an earthquake, but the Lord was not in the earthquake.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said: Go out and stand on the mountain before the Lord; the Lord will pass by. There was a strong and violent wind rending the mountains and crushing rocks before the Lord —but the Lord was not in the wind; after the wind, an earthquake—but the Lord was not in the earthquake;

text.NRSV: and after the earthquake a fire, but the Lord was not in the fire; and after the fire a sound of sheer silence.
text.Rotherham: And, after the earthquake, a, fire, Not, in the fire, was, Yahweh,—And, after the fire, the voice of a gentle whisper.
text.KJV: 19:12 And after the earthquake, a fire, but the Lord was not in the fire: and after the fire, a still small voice.
text.NABRE: after the earthquake, fire—but the Lord was not in the fire; after the fire, a light silent sound.

text.NRSV: When Elijah heard it, he wrapped his face in his mantle and went out and stood at the entrance of the cave. Then there came a voice to him that said, “What are you doing here, Elijah?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Elijah heard it, he wrapped his face in his mantle, and went forth, and stood at the entrance of the cave,—and lo! unto him, a voice; and it said—What doest thou here, Elijah?
text.KJV: 19:13 And it was so, when Eliiah heard it, that he wrapped his face in his mantle, and went out, and stood in the entring in of the caue: and behold, there came a voice vnto him, and sayd, What doest thou here, Eliiah?
text.NABRE: When he heard this, Elijah hid his face in his cloak and went out and stood at the entrance of the cave. A voice said to him, Why are you here, Elijah?

text.NRSV: He answered, “I have been very zealous for the Lord, the God of hosts; for the Israelites have forsaken your covenant, thrown down your altars, and killed your prophets with the sword. I alone am left, and they are seeking my life, to take it away.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—Very jealous, have I been, for Yahweh, God of hosts, because the sons of Israel have forsaken thy covenant, thine altars, have they thrown down, thy prophets also, have they slain with the sword,—and, I alone, am left, and they rove sought my life, to take it.
text.KJV: 19:14 And he sayd, I haue beene very iealous for the Lord God of hostes, because the children of Israel haue forsaken thy Couenant, throwen downe thine Altars, and slaine thy Prophets with the sword, and I, euen I onely am left, and they seeke my life, to take it away.
text.NABRE: He replied, “I have been most zealous for the Lord, the God of hosts, but the Israelites have forsaken your covenant. They have destroyed your altars and murdered your prophets by the sword. I alone remain, and they seek to take my life.”

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to him, “Go, return on your way to the wilderness of Damascus; when you arrive, you shall anoint Hazael as king over Aram.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto him, Go, return to thy way, towards the wilderness of Damascus,—and, when thou enterest, then shalt thou anoint Hazael to be king over Syria;
text.KJV: 19:15 And the Lord sayd vnto him, Goe, returne on thy way to the wildernesse of Damascus: and when thou commest, anoint Hazael to be King ouer Syria.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to him: Go back! Take the desert road to Damascus. When you arrive, you shall anoint Hazael as king of Aram.

text.NRSV: Also you shall anoint Jehu son of Nimshi as king over Israel; and you shall anoint Elisha son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah as prophet in your place.
text.Rotherham: And, Jehu son of Minshi, shalt thou anoint to be king over Israel,—and, Elisha son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah, shalt thou anoint to be prophet in thy stead:
text.KJV: 19:16 And Iehu the sonne of Nimshi shalt thou anoint to bee king ouer Israel: and Elisha the sonne of Shaphat of Abel Meholah, shalt thou annoint to be Prophet in thy roume.
text.NABRE: You shall also anoint Jehu, son of Nimshi, as king of Israel, and Elisha, son of Shaphat of Abel-meholah, as prophet to succeed you.

text.NRSV: Whoever escapes from the sword of Hazael, Jehu shall kill; and whoever escapes from the sword of Jehu, Elisha shall kill.
text.Rotherham: Then shall it come to pass, that—him that escapeth the sword of Hazael, shall, Jehu, slay,—and, him that escapeth the sword of Jehu, shall, Elisha, slay;
text.KJV: 19:17 And it shall come to passe, that him that escapeth the sword of Hazael, shall Iehu slay: and him that escapeth from the sword of Iehu, shall Elisha slay.
text.NABRE: Anyone who escapes the sword of Hazael, Jehu will kill. Anyone who escapes the sword of Jehu, Elisha will kill.

text.NRSV: Yet I will leave seven thousand in Israel, all the knees that have not bowed to Baal, and every mouth that has not kissed him.”
text.Rotherham: Yet will I leave remaining in Israel, seven thousand,—all knees which have not bowed to Baal, and all mouths which have not kissed to him.
text.KJV: 19:18 Yet I haue left me seuen thousand in Israel, all the knees which haue not bowed vnto Baal, and euery mouth which hath not kissed him.
text.NABRE: But I will spare seven thousand in Israel—every knee that has not bent to Baal, every mouth that has not kissed him.

text.NRSV: So he set out from there, and found Elisha son of Shaphat, who was plowing. There were twelve yoke of oxen ahead of him, and he was with the twelfth. Elijah passed by him and threw his mantle over him.
text.Rotherham: So he departed from thence, and found Elisha son of Shaphat, as he was plowing, with twelve yoke of oxen before him, he being with the twelfth,—so Elijah crossed over unto him, and cast his mantle towards him;
text.KJV: 19:19 ¶ So hee departed thence and found Elisha the sonne of Shaphat, who was plowing with twelue yoke of oxen before him, and hee with the twelfth: and Eliiah passed by him, and cast his mantle vpon him.
text.NABRE: Elijah set out, and came upon Elisha, son of Shaphat, as he was plowing with twelve yoke of oxen; he was following the twelfth. Elijah went over to him and threw his cloak on him.

text.NRSV: He left the oxen, ran after Elijah, and said, “Let me kiss my father and my mother, and then I will follow you.” Then Elijah said to him, “Go back again; for what have I done to you?”
text.Rotherham: and he left the oxen, and ran after Elijah, and said—Let me, I pray thee, kiss my father and my mother, that I may follow thee. And he said unto him,—Go, turn back, for what have I done to thee?
text.KJV: 19:20 And he left the oxen, and ranne after Eliiah, and said, Let mee, I pray thee, kisse my father and my mother, and then I wil follow thee: and he said vnto him, Goe backe againe; for what haue I done to thee?
text.NABRE: Elisha left the oxen, ran after Elijah, and said, “Please, let me kiss my father and mother good-bye, and I will follow you.” Elijah answered, “Go back! What have I done to you?”

text.NRSV: He returned from following him, took the yoke of oxen, and slaughtered them; using the equipment from the oxen, he boiled their flesh, and gave it to the people, and they ate. Then he set out and followed Elijah, and became his servant.
text.Rotherham: So he turned back from following him, and took the yoke of oxen, and sacrificed them, and, with the implements of the oxen, boiled their flesh, and gave unto the people, and they did eat,—then he arose, and followed Elijah, and ministered unto him.
text.KJV: 19:21 And he returned backe from him, and tooke a yoke of oxen, & slew them, and boyled their flesh with the instruments of the oxen, and gaue vnto the people, and they did eat: then he arose, and went after Eliiah, and ministred vnto him.
text.NABRE: Elisha left him and, taking the yoke of oxen, slaughtered them; he used the plowing equipment for fuel to boil their flesh, and gave it to the people to eat. Then he left and followed Elijah to serve him.

text.NRSV: King Ben-hadad of Aram gathered all his army together; thirty-two kings were with him, along with horses and chariots. He marched up and besieged Samaria, and fought against it.
text.Rotherham: Now, Ben-hadad, king of Syria, had gathered together all his forces, and, thirty-two kings, were with him, and horses and chariots,—then came he up, and laid siege to Samaria, and made war against it.
text.KJV: 20:1 And Benhadad the King of Syria gathered all his hoste together, and there were thirtie and two kings with him, and horses, and charets: and hee went vp and besieged Samaria, and warred against it.
text.NABRE: V. The Story of Ahab Chapter 20 Ahab’s Victories over Aram. Ben-hadad, king of Aram, gathered all his forces and, accompanied by thirty-two kings with horses and chariotry, set out to besiege and attack Samaria.

text.NRSV: He sent messengers into the city to King Ahab of Israel, and said to him: “Thus says Ben-hadad:
text.Rotherham: And he sent messengers unto Ahab king of Israel, into the city, and said to him,—Thus, saith Ben-hadad,
text.KJV: 20:2 And hee sent messengers to Ahab king of Israel, into the city, and saide vnto him, Thus saith Benhadad,
text.NABRE: He sent messengers to Ahab, king of Israel, within the city,

text.NRSV: ‘Your silver and gold are mine; your fairest wives and children also are mine.’”
text.Rotherham: Thy silver and thy gold, are, mine, and, thy wives and thy sons, the goodliest, are, mine.
text.KJV: 20:3 Thy siluer and thy gold is mine, thy wiues also, and thy children, euen the goodliest, are mine.
text.NABRE: and said to him, “This is Ben-hadad’s message: ‘Your silver and gold are mine, and your wives and your fine children are mine.’”

text.NRSV: The king of Israel answered, “As you say, my lord, O king, I am yours, and all that I have.”
text.Rotherham: Then responded the king of Israel, and said, According to thy word, my lord O king! thine, am I, and all that I have.
text.KJV: 20:4 And the king of Israel answered, and said, My lord O king, according to thy saying, I am thine, and all that I haue.
text.NABRE: The king of Israel answered, “Just as you say, my lord king, I and all I have are yours.”

text.NRSV: The messengers came again and said: “Thus says Ben-hadad: ‘I sent to you, saying, “Deliver to me your silver and gold, your wives and children”;
text.Rotherham: And the messengers came back again, and said, Thus, speaketh Ben-hadad, saying,—Because I sent unto thee, saying, Thy silver and thy gold and thy wives and thy sons, to me, shalt thou give,
text.KJV: 20:5 And the messengers came againe, and saide, Thus speaketh Benhadad, saying, Although I haue sent vnto thee, saying, Thou shalt deliuer me thy siluer, and thy gold, and thy wiues, and thy children:
text.NABRE: But the messengers came again and said, “This is Ben-hadad’s message: ‘I sent you word: Give me your silver and gold, your wives and your children.

text.NRSV: nevertheless I will send my servants to you tomorrow about this time, and they shall search your house and the houses of your servants, and lay hands on whatever pleases them and take it away.’”
text.Rotherham: Therefore, about this time to-morrow, will I send my servants unto thee, and they shall search thy house, and the houses of thy servants,—and it shall be, that, all the delight of thine eyes, shall they put in their hand, and take away.
text.KJV: 20:6 Yet I will send my seruants vnto thee to morrow about this time, and they shall search thine house, and the houses of thy seruants; and it shall be, that whatsoeuer is pleasant in thine eies, they shall put it in their hand, and take it away.
text.NABRE: But now I say: At this time tomorrow I will send my servants to you, and they shall ransack your house and the houses of your servants. They shall seize and take away whatever you consider valuable.’”

text.NRSV: Then the king of Israel called all the elders of the land, and said, “Look now! See how this man is seeking trouble; for he sent to me for my wives, my children, my silver, and my gold, and I did not refuse him.”
text.Rotherham: Then called the king of Israel, for all the elders of the land, and said—Mark, I pray you, and see, how this man is seeking, mischief,—for he had sent unto me, for my wives, and for my sons, and for my silver, and for my gold, and I refused him not.
text.KJV: 20:7 Then the king of Israel called all the Elders of the land, and saide; Marke, I pray you, and see how this man seeketh mischiefe: for hee sent vnto me for my wiues, and for my children, and for my siluer, and for my gold, and I denied him not.
text.NABRE: The king of Israel then summoned all the elders of the land and said: “Understand clearly that this man is intent on evil. When he sent to me for my wives and children, my silver and my gold, I did not refuse him.”

text.NRSV: Then all the elders and all the people said to him, “Do not listen, or consent.”
text.Rotherham: And all the elders and all the people said unto him,—Do not thou hearken, neither do thou consent.
text.KJV: 20:8 And all the Elders, and all the people said vnto him; Hearken not vnto him, nor consent.
text.NABRE: All the elders and all the people said to him, “Do not listen. Do not give in.”

text.NRSV: So he said to the messengers of Ben-hadad, “Tell my lord the king: All that you first demanded of your servant I will do; but this thing I cannot do.” The messengers left and brought him word again.
text.Rotherham: So he said unto the messengers of Ben-hadad—Say ye to my lord the king,—All that thou didst send for, to thy servant at the first, will I do, but, this thing, I cannot do. And the messengers departed, and took him back word.
text.KJV: 20:9 Wherefore hee said vnto the messengers of Benhadad, Tell my lord the king, All that thou diddest send for to thy seruant at the first, I will doe: but this thing I may not doe. And the messengers departed, and brought him word againe.
text.NABRE: Accordingly he directed the messengers of Ben-hadad, “Say this: ‘To my lord the king: I will do all that you demanded of your servant the first time. But this I cannot do.’” The messengers left and reported this.

text.NRSV: Ben-hadad sent to him and said, “The gods do so to me, and more also, if the dust of Samaria shall sufficient for handfuls for all the people who follow me.”
text.Rotherham: Then Ben-hadad sent unto him and said,—So, let the gods do to me, and, so, let them add,—if the dust of Samaria suffice by handfuls, for all the people who are at my feet.
text.KJV: 20:10 And Benhadad sent vnto him, and said, The gods doe so vnto me and more also, if the dust of Samaria shall suffice for handfuls for all the people that follow me.
text.NABRE: Ben-hadad then responded, “May the gods do thus to me and more, if there will remain enough dust in Samaria to make handfuls for all my followers.”

text.NRSV: The king of Israel answered, “Tell him: One who puts on armor should not boast like one who takes it off.”
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel responded and said: Tell him,—Let not, him that girdeth, boast himself like him, that looseneth.
text.KJV: 20:11 And the king of Israel answered, and said, Tell him, Let not him that girdeth on his harnesse, boast himselfe, as he that putteth it off.
text.NABRE: The king of Israel replied, “Tell him, ‘Let not one who puts on armor boast like one who takes it off.’”

text.NRSV: When Ben-hadad heard this message—he was drinking with the kings in the booths—he said to his men, “Take your positions!” And they took their positions against the city.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when he heard this message, as, he, was drinking, he and the kings in the pavilions, that he said unto his servants—Make ready! So they made ready, against the city.
text.KJV: 20:12 And it came to passe, when Benhadad heard this message (as hee was drinking, he and the kings in the pauilions) that hee said vnto his seruants, Set yourselues in aray. And they set themselues in aray against the citie.
text.NABRE: Ben-hadad was drinking in the pavilions with the kings when he heard this reply. He commanded his servants, “Get ready!”; and they got ready to storm the city.

text.NRSV: Then a certain prophet came up to King Ahab of Israel and said, “Thus says the Lord, Have you seen all this great multitude? Look, I will give it into your hand today; and you shall know that I am the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And lo! a certain prophet, drew near unto Ahab king of Israel, and said,—Thus, saith Yahweh, Hast thou seen all this great multitude? Behold me! delivering it into thy hand, to-day, so shalt thou know that, I, am, Yahweh.
text.KJV: 20:13 ¶ And behold, there came a Prophet vnto Ahab king of Israel, saying, Thus saith the Lord, Hast thou seene all this great multitude? behold, I will deliuer it into thine hand this day, and thou shalt knowe that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then a prophet came up to Ahab, king of Israel, and said: “The Lord says, Do you see all this vast army? Today I am giving it into your power, that you may know that I am the Lord.”

text.NRSV: Ahab said, “By whom?” He said, “Thus says the Lord, By the young men who serve the district governors.” Then he said, “Who shall begin the battle?” He answered, “You.”
text.Rotherham: And Ahab said—By whom? And he said—Thus, saith Yahweh, By the young men of the princes of the provinces. Then said he—Who shall begin the war? And he said—Thou!
text.KJV: 20:14 And Ahab saide, By whom? and he saide, Thus saith the Lord, Euen by the young men of the Princes of the prouinces: Then he said, Who shall order the battell? And hee answered, Thou.
text.NABRE: But Ahab asked, “Through whom will it be given over?” He answered, “The Lord says, Through the aides of the provincial governors.” Then Ahab asked, “Who is to attack?” He replied, “You are.”

text.NRSV: Then he mustered the young men who served the district governors, two hundred thirty-two; after them he mustered all the people of Israel, seven thousand.
text.Rotherham: Then numbered he the young men of the princes of the provinces, and they were found to be, two hundred and thirty-two,—and, after them, he numbered all the people all the sons of Israel, seven thousand.
text.KJV: 20:15 Then he numbred the young men of the Princes of the prouinces, and they were two hundred and thirty two: and after them hee numbred all the people, euen all the children of Israel, being seuen thousand.
text.NABRE: So Ahab mustered the aides of the provincial governors, two hundred thirty-two of them. Behind them he mustered all the Israelite soldiery, who numbered seven thousand in all.

text.NRSV: They went out at noon, while Ben-hadad was drinking himself drunk in the booths, he and the thirty-two kings who helped him.
text.Rotherham: And they went forth at noon. Now, Ben-hadad, was drinking himself drunk, in the pavilions, he and the thirty-two kings helping him.
text.KJV: 20:16 And they went out at noone: But Benhadad was drinking himselfe drunke in the pauilions, hee and the kings, the thirty and two kings that helped him.
text.NABRE: They marched out at noon, while Ben-hadad was drinking heavily in the pavilions with the thirty-two kings who were his allies.

text.NRSV: The young men who served the district governors went out first. Ben-hadad had sent out scouts, and they reported to him, “Men have come out from Samaria.”
text.Rotherham: Then went forth the young men of the princes of the provinces, first,—and, when Ben-hadad sent, they told him, saying—Men, have come forth, out of Samaria.
text.KJV: 20:17 And the young men of the Princes of the Prouinces went out first, and Benhadad sent out, and they told him, saying, There are men come out of Samaria.
text.NABRE: When the aides of the provincial governors marched out first, Ben-hadad received word, “Some men have marched out of Samaria.”

text.NRSV: He said, “If they have come out for peace, take them alive; if they have come out for war, take them alive.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—If, peaceably, they have come, take them alive,—or, if, fighting, they have come, alive, take ye them.
text.KJV: 20:18 And he said, Whether they be come out for peace, take them aliue: or whether they be come out for warre, take them aliue.
text.NABRE: He answered, “Whether they have come out for peace or for war, take them alive.”

text.NRSV: But these had already come out of the city: the young men who served the district governors, and the army that followed them.
text.Rotherham: Now, when, these, had come forth out of the city, even the young men of the princes of the provinces,—with the force which was following them,
text.KJV: 20:19 So these yong men of the princes of the prouinces, came out of the citie, and the armie which followed them:
text.NABRE: But when these had come out of the city—the aides of the provincial governors with the army following them—

text.NRSV: Each killed his man; the Arameans fled and Israel pursued them, but King Ben-hadad of Aram escaped on a horse with the cavalry.
text.Rotherham: then smote they every one his man, and the Syrians fled, and Israel pursued them,—but Ben-hadad king of Syria escaped on horse, with horsemen.
text.KJV: 20:20 And they slew euery one his man: and the Syrians fled, and Israel pursued them: and Benhadad the king of Syria escaped on an horse, with the horsemen.
text.NABRE: each of them struck down his man. The Arameans fled with Israel pursuing them, while Ben-hadad, king of Aram, escaped on a chariot horse.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel went out, captured the horses and chariots, and defeated the Arameans with a great slaughter.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel went forth, and took the horses and the chariots,—and he went on smiting the Syrians, with a great smiting.
text.KJV: 20:21 And the king of Israel went out, and smote the horses and charets, and slewe the Syrians with a great slaughter.
text.NABRE: Then the king of Israel went out and destroyed the horses and chariots. Thus he inflicted a severe defeat on Aram.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet approached the king of Israel and said to him, “Go, strengthen yourself, and consider well what you have to do; for in the spring the king of Aram will come up against you.”
text.Rotherham: Then drew near the prophet, unto the king of Israel, and said unto him—Go strengthen thyself, and mark and see, what thou wilt do,—for, at the return of the year, is, the king of Syria, coming up against thee.
text.KJV: 20:22 ¶ And the Prophet came to the king of Israel, and said vnto him, Goe, strengthen thy selfe, and marke and see what thou doest: for at the returne of the yeere, the king of Syria will come vp against thee.
text.NABRE: Then the prophet approached the king of Israel and said to him: “Go, regroup your forces. Understand clearly what you must do, for at the turning of the year the king of Aram will attack you.”

text.NRSV: The servants of the king of Aram said to him, “Their gods are gods of the hills, and so they were stronger than we; but let us fight against them in the plain, and surely we shall be stronger than they.
text.Rotherham: And, the servants of the king of Syria, said unto him,—Gods of the mountains, are their gods, for this cause, prevailed they against us,—but, only let us fight with them in the plain, and verily we shall prevail against them.
text.KJV: 20:23 And the seruants of the King of Syria said vnto him, Their gods are gods of the hilles, therefore they were stronger then wee: but let vs fight against them in the plaine, and surely we shall be stronger then they.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile the servants of the king of Aram said to him: “Their gods are mountain gods. That is why they defeated us. But if we fight them on level ground, we shall be sure to defeat them.

text.NRSV: Also do this: remove the kings, each from his post, and put commanders in their places;
text.Rotherham: But, this thing, do,—set aside the kings, every man out of his place, and put governors in their stead;
text.KJV: 20:24 And doe this thing, Take the kings away, euery man out of his place, and put captaines in their roumes.
text.NABRE: This is what you must do: Take the kings from their posts and put prefects in their places.

text.NRSV: and muster an army like the army that you have lost, horse for horse, and chariot for chariot; then we will fight against them in the plain, and surely we shall be stronger than they.” He heeded their voice, and did so.
text.Rotherham: and, thou, must number thee a force, like the force which thou hast lost, both horse for horse and chariot for chariot, and, if we fight with them in the plain, verily we shall prevail against them. And he hearkened unto their voice, and did so.
text.KJV: 20:25 And number thee an armie, like the armie that thou hast lost, horse for horse, and charet for charet: and wee will fight against them in the plaine, and surely wee shall be stronger then they. And hee hearkened vnto their voice, and did so.
text.NABRE: Raise an army as large as the army you have lost, horse for horse, chariot for chariot. Let us fight them on level ground, and we shall surely defeat them.” He took their advice and did this.

text.NRSV: In the spring Ben-hadad mustered the Arameans and went up to Aphek to fight against Israel.
text.Rotherham: And so it came to pass at the return of the year, that Ben-hadad numbered the Syrians,—and came up to Aphek, to fight with Israel;
text.KJV: 20:26 And it came to passe at the returne of the yeere, that Benhadad numbred the Syrians, and went vp to Aphek, to fight against Israel.
text.NABRE: At the turning of the year, Ben-hadad mustered Aram and went up to Aphek to fight against Israel.

text.NRSV: After the Israelites had been mustered and provisioned, they went out to meet them; the Israelites encamped before them like two little flocks of goats, while the Arameans filled the country.
text.Rotherham: and, the sons of Israel, were numbered, and provisioned, and went to meet them,—and the sons of Israel encamped before them like two little flocks of goats, whereas, the Syrians, filled the land.
text.KJV: 20:27 And the children of Israel were numbred, and were all present, and went against them: and the children of Israel pitched before them, like two little flockes of kids: but the Syrians filled the countrey.
text.NABRE: The Israelites, too, were mustered and supplied with provisions; then they went out to meet the enemy. The Israelites, encamped opposite, looked like little flocks of goats, while Aram covered the land.

text.NRSV: A man of God approached and said to the king of Israel, “Thus says the Lord, Because the Arameans have said, ‘The Lord is a god of the hills but he is not a god of the valleys,’ therefore I will give all this great multitude into your hand, and you shall know that I am the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Then approached the man of God, and spake unto the king of Israel, and said—Thus, saith Yahweh—Because the Syrians have said—A god of the mountains, is Yahweh, but, not a god of the vales, is he, therefore will I deliver all this great multitude, into thy hand, so shalt thou know, that, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: 20:28 ¶ And there came a man of God, and spake vnto the king of Israel, and sayd, Thus sayth the Lord, Because the Syrians haue sayde, The Lord is God of the hilles, but hee is not God of the valleys: therefore will I deliuer all this great multitude into thine hand, and yee shall know that I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: A man of God approached and said to the king of Israel: “The Lord says, Because Aram has said the Lord is a god of mountains, not a god of plains, I will give all this vast army into your power that you may know I am the Lord.”

text.NRSV: They encamped opposite each other seven days. Then on the seventh day the battle was joined; the Israelites killed one hundred thousand Aramean foot soldiers in one day.
text.Rotherham: So they encamped, these, over against, those, seven days,—and it came to pass, on the seventh day, that the battle was joined, and the sons of Israel smote the Syrians, a hundred thousand footmen, in one day.
text.KJV: 20:29 And they pitched one ouer against the other seuen daies, and so it was, that in the seuenth day the battell was ioyned: and the children of Israel slewe of the Syrians an hundred thousand footmen in one day.
text.NABRE: They were encamped opposite each other for seven days. On the seventh day battle was joined, and the Israelites struck down one hundred thousand foot soldiers of Aram in one day.

text.NRSV: The rest fled into the city of Aphek; and the wall fell on twenty-seven thousand men who were left. Ben-hadad also fled, and entered the city to hide in an inner chamber.
text.Rotherham: And they who were left fled to Aphek, into the city, and the wall fell upon twenty-seven thousand men who were left,—and, Ben-hadad, fled, and came into the city, into a chamber within a chamber.
text.KJV: 20:30 But the rest fled to Aphek, into the citie, and there a wall fell vpon twentie and seuen thousand of the men that were left: and Benhadad fled, and came into the citie, into an inner chamber.
text.NABRE: The survivors fled into the city of Aphek, where the wall collapsed on twenty-seven thousand of them. Ben-hadad, too, fled, and took refuge within the city, in an inner room.

text.NRSV: His servants said to him, “Look, we have heard that the kings of the house of Israel are merciful kings; let us put sackcloth around our waists and ropes on our heads, and go out to the king of Israel; perhaps he will spare your life.”
text.Rotherham: And his servants said unto him, Lo! we pray thee, we have heard, of the kings of the house of Israel, that, kings known for lovingkindness, they are. Let us, we pray thee, put sackcloth upon our loins, and ropes about our head, and let us go forth unto the king of Israel, peradventure he will save alive thy soul.
text.KJV: 20:31 ¶ And his seruants said vnto him, Behold now, wee haue heard that the kings of the house of Israel are mercifull kings: let vs, I pray thee, put sackcloth on our loines, and ropes vpon our heads, and goe out to the king of Israel; peraduenture he will saue thy life.
text.NABRE: His servants said to him: “We have heard that the kings of the house of Israel are merciful kings. Allow us, therefore, to garb ourselves in sackcloth, with cords around our heads, and go out to the king of Israel. Perhaps he will spare your life.”

text.NRSV: So they tied sackcloth around their waists and put ropes on their heads, went to the king of Israel, and said, “Your servant Ben-hadad says, ‘Please let me live.’” And he said, “Is he still alive? He is my brother.”
text.Rotherham: So they girded sackcloth upon their loins, and [put] ropes about their heads, and came in unto the king of Israel, and said, Thy servant, Ben-hadad, saith,—Let my soul live, I pray thee. And he said,—Is he yet alive? My brother, he is.
text.KJV: 20:32 So they girded sackcloth on their loynes, and put ropes on their heads, and came to the king of Israel, and said, Thy seruant Benhadad saith, I pray thee, let me liue. And he said, Is he yet aliue? he is my brother.
text.NABRE: Dressed in sackcloth girded at the waist and wearing cords around their heads, they went to the king of Israel and said, “Your servant Ben-hadad says, ‘Spare my life!’” He asked, “Is he still alive? He is my brother.”

text.NRSV: Now the men were watching for an omen; they quickly took it up from him and said, “Yes, Ben-hadad is your brother.” Then he said, “Go and bring him.” So Ben-hadad came out to him; and he had him come up into the chariot.
text.Rotherham: Now, the men, could divine, so they hastened to let him confirm the word of his own accord, and they said,—Thy brother, is Ben-hadad! He said therefore,—Go fetch him. So Ben-hadad came forth unto him, and he made him come up unto him on his chariot.
text.KJV: 20:33 Now the men did diligently obserue whether any thing would come from him, and did hastily catch it: and they saide, Thy brother Benhadad. Then he said, Goe ye, bring him: then Benhadad came forth to him: and hee caused him to come vp into the charet.
text.NABRE: Hearing this as a good omen, the men quickly took him at his word and said, “Ben-hadad is your brother.” He answered, “Go and get him.” When Ben-hadad came out to him, the king had him mount his chariot.

text.NRSV: Ben-hadad said to him, “The cities that my father took from your father I will restore; and you may establish bazaars for yourself in Damascus, as my father did in Samaria.” To which Ahab replied, “I will let you go on these terms.” So he made a treaty with him and let him go.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him—The cities which my father took from thy father, will I restore, and, bazaars, shalt thou make thee in Damascus, as my father made in Samaria. So then, I, with this covenant, will let thee go. So he solemnised with him a covenant, and let him go.
