text.KJV: 20:34 And Benhadad said vnto him, The cities which my father tooke from thy father, I will restore, and thou shalt make streets for thee in Damascus, as my father made in Samaria. Then, said Ahab, I will send thee away with this couenant. So he made a couenant with him, and sent him away.
text.NABRE: Ben-hadad said to him, “The cities my father took from your father I will restore, and you may set up bazaars for yourself in Damascus, as my father did in Samaria.” Ahab replied, “For my part, I will set you free on those terms.” So he made a covenant with him and then set him free.

text.NRSV: At the command of the Lord a certain member of a company of prophets said to another, “Strike me!” But the man refused to strike him.
text.Rotherham: And, a certain man of the sons of the prophets, said unto his neighbour, by the word of Yahweh—Smite me, I pray thee. But the man refused to smite him.
text.KJV: 20:35 ¶ And a certaine man of the sonnes of the Prophets, saide vnto his neighbour in the word of the Lord, Smite me, I pray thee. And the man refused to smite him.
text.NABRE: Prophetic Condemnation. Acting on the word of the Lord, one of the guild prophets said to his companion, “Strike me.” But he refused to strike him.

text.NRSV: Then he said to him, “Because you have not obeyed the voice of the Lord, as soon as you have left me, a lion shall kill you.” And when he had left him, a lion met him and killed him.
text.Rotherham: So he said to him—Because thou hast not hearkened unto the voice of Yahweh, lo! when thou art departing from me, there shall smite thee a lion. And when he departed from beside him, a lion found him, and smote him.
text.KJV: 20:36 Then said he vnto him, Because thou hast not obeyed the voyce of the Lord, beholde, assoone as thou art departed from me, a lyon shal slay thee. And assoone as hee was departed from him, a lyon found him, and slew him.
text.NABRE: Then he said to him, “Since you did not obey the voice of the Lord, a lion will attack you when you leave me.” When he left him, a lion came upon him and attacked him.

text.NRSV: Then he found another man and said, “Strike me!” So the man struck him, striking and wounding him.
text.Rotherham: Then found he another man, and said,—Smite me, I pray thee. So the man smote him—kept on smiting and wounding.
text.KJV: 20:37 Then he found another man, and said, Smite me, I pray thee. And the man smote him, so that in smiting hee wounded him.
text.NABRE: Then the prophet met another man and said, “Strike me.” The man struck him a blow and wounded him.

text.NRSV: So the prophet departed, and waited for the king on the road, disguising himself with a bandage over his eyes.
text.Rotherham: Then the prophet departed, and waited for the king, by the way,—and disguised himself with his turban over his eyes.
text.KJV: 20:38 So the prophet departed, and waited for the king by the way, and disguised himselfe with ashes vpon his face.
text.NABRE: The prophet went on and waited for the king on the road, disguising himself with a bandage over his eyes.

text.NRSV: As the king passed by, he cried to the king and said, “Your servant went out into the thick of the battle; then a soldier turned and brought a man to me and said, ‘Guard this man; if for any reason he is missing, your life shall be for his life, or else you shall pay a talent of silver.’
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when, the king, was passing, he, cried out unto the king,—and said—Thy servant, went out in the midst of the battle, and lo! a man, turned aside and brought unto me a man, and said—Keep this man, if he be, missing, then shall, thy life, go for, his life, or, a talent of silver, shalt thou weigh out.
text.KJV: 20:39 And as the king passed by, he cried vnto the king: and he saide, Thy seruant went out into the mids of the battell, and behold, a man turned aside, and brought a man vnto me, and said, Keep this man: if by any meanes he be missing, then shall thy life be for his life, or else thou shalt pay a talent of siluer.
text.NABRE: As the king was passing, he called out to the king and said: “Your servant went into the thick of the battle, and suddenly someone turned and brought me a man and said, ‘Guard this man. If he is missing, you shall have to pay for his life with your life or pay out a talent of silver.’

text.NRSV: While your servant was busy here and there, he was gone.” The king of Israel said to him, “So shall your judgment be; you yourself have decided it.”
text.Rotherham: And so it was, as thy servant was busy here and there, that, he, was gone. And the king of Israel said unto him—Such, is thy judgment, thou thyself, hast decided it.
text.KJV: 20:40 And as thy seruant was busie here and there, he was gone. And the king of Israel saide vnto him, So shall thy iudgement bee, thy selfe hast discided it.
text.NABRE: But while your servant was occupied here and there, the man disappeared.” The king of Israel said to him, “That is your sentence. You have decided it yourself.”

text.NRSV: Then he quickly took the bandage away from his eyes. The king of Israel recognized him as one of the prophets.
text.Rotherham: Then hastened he, and removed the turban from over his eyes,—and the king of Israel knew him, that, of the prophets, was, he.
text.KJV: 20:41 And he hasted, and tooke the ashes away from his face, and the king of Israel discerned him that hee was of the Prophets.
text.NABRE: He quickly removed the bandage from his eyes, and the king of Israel recognized him as one of the prophets.

text.NRSV: Then he said to him, “Thus says the Lord, Because you have let the man go whom I had devoted to destruction, therefore your life shall be for his life, and your people for his people.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh, Because thou hast let go the man whom I had devoted, out of thy hand, therefore shall, thy life, be instead of, his life, and, thy people, instead of, his people.
text.KJV: 20:42 And hee said vnto him, Thus saith the Lord, Because thou hast let goe out of thy hand, a man whom I appointed to vtter destruction, therfore thy life shall goe for his life, and thy people for his people.
text.NABRE: He said to him: “The Lord says, Because you have set free the man I put under the ban, your life shall pay for his life, your people for his people.”

text.NRSV: The king of Israel set out for his house, resentful and sullen, and came to Samaria.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel departed unto his house, sullen and disturbed,—and entered Samaria.
text.KJV: 20:43 And the king of Israel went to his house, heauie, and displeased, and came to Samaria.
text.NABRE: Disturbed and angry, the king of Israel set off for home and entered Samaria.

text.NRSV: Later the following events took place: Naboth the Jezreelite had a vineyard in Jezreel, beside the palace of King Ahab of Samaria.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, after these things, that Naboth the Jezreelite had, a vineyard, which was in Jezreel,—hard by the palace of Ahab, king of Samaria.
text.KJV: 21:1 And it came to passe after these things, that Naboth the Iezreelite had a vineyard, which was in Iezreel, hard by the palace of Ahab king of Samaria.
text.NABRE: Chapter 21 Seizure of Naboth’s Vineyard. Naboth the Jezreelite had a vineyard in Jezreel next to the palace of Ahab, king of Samaria. Some time later,

text.NRSV: And Ahab said to Naboth, “Give me your vineyard, so that I may have it for a vegetable garden, because it is near my house; I will give you a better vineyard for it; or, if it seems good to you, I will give you its value in money.”
text.Rotherham: Ahab, therefore, spake unto Naboth, saying—Come! give me thy vineyard, that I may have it for a garden of herbs, for, the same, is near by my house, and let me give thee, instead thereof, a vineyard better than it,—if it be good in thine eyes, I will give thee silver to the value of this.
text.KJV: 21:2 And Ahab spake vnto Naboth, saying, Giue me thy vineyard, that I may haue it for a garden of herbes, because it is neere vnto my house, and I will giue thee for it a better vineyard then it: or if it seeme good to thee, I will giue thee the worth of it in money.
text.NABRE: Ahab said to Naboth, “Give me your vineyard to be my vegetable garden, since it is close by, next to my house. I will give you a better vineyard in exchange, or, if you prefer, I will give you its value in money.”

text.NRSV: But Naboth said to Ahab, “The Lord forbid that I should give you my ancestral inheritance.”
text.Rotherham: And Naboth said unto Ahab,—Far be it from me, of Yahweh! that I should give up the inheritance of my fathers, unto thee!
text.KJV: 21:3 And Naboth said to Ahab, The Lord forbid it mee, that I should giue the inheritance of my fathers vnto thee.
text.NABRE: Naboth said to Ahab, “The Lord forbid that I should give you my ancestral heritage.”

text.NRSV: Ahab went home resentful and sullen because of what Naboth the Jezreelite had said to him; for he had said, “I will not give you my ancestral inheritance.” He lay down on his bed, turned away his face, and would not eat.
text.Rotherham: So Ahab came into his house, sullen and disturbed, because of the word which Naboth the Jezreelite had spoken unto him, that he should have said, I will not give thee the inheritance of my fathers. So he laid him down upon his bed, and turned away his face, and did eat no food.
text.KJV: 21:4 And Ahab came into his house, heauie, and displeased, because of the word which Naboth the Iezreelite had spoken to him: for he had saide, I will not giue thee the inheritance of my fathers: and he laid him downe vpon his bed, and turned away his face, and would eate no bread.
text.NABRE: Ahab went home disturbed and angry at the answer Naboth the Jezreelite had given him: “I will not give you my ancestral heritage.” Lying down on his bed, he turned away and would not eat.

text.NRSV: His wife Jezebel came to him and said, “Why are you so depressed that you will not eat?”
text.Rotherham: Then came unto him Jezebel his wife,—and said unto him—Why is it, that thy spirit is sullen, that thou art, not eating food?
text.KJV: 21:5 ¶ But Iezebel his wife came to him, and said vnto him, Why is thy spirit so sad, that thou eatest no bread?
text.NABRE: His wife Jezebel came to him and said to him, “Why are you so sullen that you will not eat?”

text.NRSV: He said to her, “Because I spoke to Naboth the Jezreelite and said to him, ‘Give me your vineyard for money; or else, if you prefer, I will give you another vineyard for it’; but he answered, ‘I will not give you my vineyard.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto her—Because I spake unto Naboth, the Jezreelite, and said unto him—Come! give me thy vineyard for silver, or, if thou wouldst prefer, I will give thee a vineyard, in its stead; and he said—I will not give thee my vineyard.
text.KJV: 21:6 And he said vnto her, Because I spake vnto Naboth the Iezreelite, and said vnto him, Giue mee thy vineyard for money, or else if it please thee, I will giue thee another vineyard for it: And he answered, I wil not giue thee my vineyard.
text.NABRE: He answered her, “Because I spoke to Naboth the Jezreelite and said to him, ‘Sell me your vineyard, or, if you prefer, I will give you a vineyard in exchange.’ But he said, ‘I will not give you my vineyard.’”

text.NRSV: His wife Jezebel said to him, “Do you now govern Israel? Get up, eat some food, and be cheerful; I will give you the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite.”
text.Rotherham: Then Jezebel his wife said unto him: Art, thou, still, going to carry on the kingdom over Israel? Rise! eat food, and let thy heart be merry, I, will give thee the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite!
text.KJV: 21:7 And Iezebel his wife saide vnto him, Doest thou now gouerne the kingdome of Israel? Arise, and eate bread, and let thine heart bee merrie: I will giue thee the vineyard of Naboth the Iezreelite.
text.NABRE: Jezebel his wife said to him, “What a king of Israel you are! Get up! Eat and be cheerful. I will give you the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite.”

text.NRSV: So she wrote letters in Ahab’s name and sealed them with his seal; she sent the letters to the elders and the nobles who lived with Naboth in his city.
text.Rotherham: So she wrote letters, in the name of Ahab, and sealed them with his signet-ring,—and sent the letters unto the elders, and unto the nobles who were in his city, dwelling with Naboth.
text.KJV: 21:8 So shee wrote letters in Ahabs name, and sealed them with his seale, and sent the letters vnto the Elders, and to the Nobles that were in his citie dwelling with Naboth.
text.NABRE: So she wrote letters in Ahab’s name and, having sealed them with his seal, sent them to the elders and to the nobles who lived in the same city with Naboth.

text.NRSV: She wrote in the letters, “Proclaim a fast, and seat Naboth at the head of the assembly;
text.Rotherham: Now she wrote in the letters, saying,—Proclaim ye a fast, and cause Naboth to sit at the head of the people;
text.KJV: 21:9 And she wrote in the letters, saying, Proclaime a fast, and set Naboth on high among the people:
text.NABRE: This is what she wrote in the letters: “Proclaim a fast and set Naboth at the head of the people.

text.NRSV: seat two scoundrels opposite him, and let them bring a charge against him, saying, ‘You have cursed God and the king.’ Then take him out, and stone him to death.”
text.Rotherham: then let two reckless men take their seats before him, that they may bear witness against him, saying, Thou hast reviled God and king! then shall ye carry him forth and stone him, that he die.
text.KJV: 21:10 And set two men, sonnes of Belial before him, to beare witnes against him, saying, Thou diddest blaspheme God and the king: and then carie him out, and stone him that he may die.
text.NABRE: Next, set two scoundrels opposite him to accuse him: ‘You have cursed God and king.’ Then take him out and stone him to death.”

text.NRSV: The men of his city, the elders and the nobles who lived in his city, did as Jezebel had sent word to them. As it was written in the letters that she had sent to them,
text.Rotherham: So the men of his city, the elders and the nobles who dwelt in his city, did just as Jezebel had sent unto them,—as it was written in the letters which she had sent unto them:
text.KJV: 21:11 And the men of his citie, euen the Elders and the Nobles who were the inhabitants in his citie, did as Iezebel had sent vnto them, and as it was written in the letters which she had sent vnto them.
text.NABRE: His fellow citizens—the elders and the nobles who dwelt in his city—did as Jezebel had ordered in the letters she sent them.

text.NRSV: they proclaimed a fast and seated Naboth at the head of the assembly.
text.Rotherham: they proclaimed a fast,—and caused Naboth to sit at the head of the people.
text.KJV: 21:12 They proclaimed a fast, and set Naboth on high among the people.
text.NABRE: They proclaimed a fast and set Naboth at the head of the people.

text.NRSV: The two scoundrels came in and sat opposite him; and the scoundrels brought a charge against Naboth, in the presence of the people, saying, “Naboth cursed God and the king.” So they took him outside the city, and stoned him to death with stones.
text.Rotherham: Then came in the two reckless men, and sat before him, and the men bare witness against him, even against Naboth, before the people, saying, Naboth hath reviled God and king! So they carried him forth, outside the city, and stoned him with stones, that he died.
text.KJV: 21:13 And there came in two men, children of Belial, and sate before him: and the men of Belial witnessed against him, euen against Naboth, in the presence of the people, saying, Naboth did blaspheme God and the king. Then they caried him foorth out of the citie, and stoned him with stones, that hee died.
text.NABRE: Two scoundrels came in and sat opposite Naboth, and the scoundrels accused him in the presence of the people, “Naboth has cursed God and king.” And they led him out of the city and stoned him to death.

text.NRSV: Then they sent to Jezebel, saying, “Naboth has been stoned; he is dead.”
text.Rotherham: Then sent they unto Jezebel, saying,—Naboth is stoned and is dead.
text.KJV: 21:14 Then they sent to Iezebel, saying, Naboth is stoned, and is dead.
text.NABRE: Then they sent word to Jezebel: “Naboth has been stoned to death.”

text.NRSV: As soon as Jezebel heard that Naboth had been stoned and was dead, Jezebel said to Ahab, “Go, take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, which he refused to give you for money; for Naboth is not alive, but dead.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Jezebel heard that Naboth was stoned and was dead, that Jezebel said unto Ahab—Rise! take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, which he refused to give thee for silver, for Naboth is not alive, but dead!
text.KJV: 21:15 ¶ And it came to passe when Iezebel heard that Naboth was stoned and was dead, that Iezebel said to Ahab, Arise, take possession of the Uineyard of Naboth the Iezreelite, which hee refused to giue thee for money: for Naboth is not aliue, but dead.
text.NABRE: When Jezebel learned that Naboth had been stoned to death, she said to Ahab, “Go, take possession of the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite which he refused to sell you, because Naboth is not alive, but dead.”

text.NRSV: As soon as Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, Ahab set out to go down to the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, to take possession of it.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, that Ahab arose, to go down unto the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, to take possession thereof.
text.KJV: 21:16 And it came to passe when Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, that Ahab rose vp to goe downe to the Uineyard of Naboth the Iezreelite, to take possession of it.
text.NABRE: When Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, he started on his way down to the vineyard of Naboth the Jezreelite, to take possession of it.

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to Elijah the Tishbite, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto Elijah the Tishbite, saying:
text.KJV: 21:17 ¶ And the word of the Lord came to Eliiah the Tishbite, saying,
text.NABRE: Prophetic Condemnation. Then the word of the Lord came to Elijah the Tishbite:

text.NRSV: “Go down to meet King Ahab of Israel, who rules in Samaria; he is now in the vineyard of Naboth, where he has gone to take possession.
text.Rotherham: Arise! go down to meet Ahab king of Israel, who is in Samaria,—lo! he is in the vineyard of Naboth, whither he hath gone down, to take possession thereof.
text.KJV: 21:18 Arise, goe downe to meet Ahab king of Israel, which is in Samaria: behold, hee is in the Uineyard of Naboth, whither he is gone downe to possesse it.
text.NABRE: Go down to meet Ahab, king of Israel, who is in Samaria. He will be in the vineyard of Naboth, where he has gone to take possession.

text.NRSV: You shall say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord: Have you killed, and also taken possession?’ You shall say to him, ‘Thus says the Lord: In the place where dogs licked up the blood of Naboth, dogs will also lick up your blood.’”
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou speak unto him, saying—Thus, saith Yahweh, Hast thou committed murder, and also taken possession? Then shalt thou speak unto him, saying: Thus, saith Yahweh, In the place where the dogs have lapped up the blood of Naboth, shall the dogs lap up thy blood, even thine.
text.KJV: 21:19 And thou shalt speake vnto him, saying, Thus saith the Lord, Hast thou killed, and also taken possession? And thou shalt speake vnto him, saying, Thus saith the Lord; In the place where dogs licked the blood of Naboth, shall dogges licke thy blood, euen thine.
text.NABRE: Tell him: “Thus says the Lord: After murdering, do you also take possession?” And tell him, “Thus says the Lord: In the place where the dogs licked up the blood of Naboth, the dogs shall lick up your blood, too.”

text.NRSV: Ahab said to Elijah, “Have you found me, O my enemy?” He answered, “I have found you. Because you have sold yourself to do what is evil in the sight of the Lord,
text.Rotherham: Then said Ahab unto Elijah, Hast thou found me, O mine enemy? And he said: I have found [thee] ! Because thou hast sold thyself to do the thing that is wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,
text.KJV: 21:20 And Ahab said to Eliiah, Hast thou found me, O mine enemie? And he answered, I haue found thee: because thou hast sold thy selfe to worke euill in the sight of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Ahab said to Elijah, “Have you found me out, my enemy?” He said, “I have found you. Because you have given yourself up to doing evil in the Lord ’s sight,

text.NRSV: I will bring disaster on you; I will consume you, and will cut off from Ahab every male, bond or free, in Israel;
text.Rotherham: Behold me! [saith he] bringing in upon thee, calamity, and I will consume after thee,—and cut off of Ahab, even the meanest, whether shut up or left at large in Israel;
text.KJV: 21:21 Behold, I will bring euill vpon thee, and will take away thy posteritie, and will cut off from Ahab him that pisseth against the wall, and him that is shut vp, and left in Israel,
text.NABRE: I am bringing evil upon you: I will consume you and will cut off every male belonging to Ahab, whether bond or free, in Israel.

text.NRSV: and I will make your house like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah, because you have provoked me to anger and have caused Israel to sin.
text.Rotherham: and will deliver up thy house—Like the house of Jeroboam son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha son of Ahijah,—for the provocation wherewith thou hast provoked, and caused, Israel, to sin.
text.KJV: 21:22 And will make thine house like the house of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha the sonne of Ahiiah, for the prouocation wherewith thou hast prouoked mee to anger, and made Israel to sinne.
text.NABRE: I will make your house like that of Jeroboam, son of Nebat, and like the house of Baasha, son of Ahijah, because you have provoked me by leading Israel into sin.”

text.NRSV: Also concerning Jezebel the Lord said, ‘The dogs shall eat Jezebel within the bounds of Jezreel.’
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, concerning Jezebel, hath Yahweh spoken, saying,—The dogs, shall eat Jezebel, in the town-land of Jezreel:
text.KJV: 21:23 And of Iezebel also spake the Lord, saying, The dogs shall eate Iezebel by the wall of Iezreel.
text.NABRE: Against Jezebel, too, the Lord declared: The dogs shall devour Jezebel in the confines of Jezreel.

text.NRSV: Anyone belonging to Ahab who dies in the city the dogs shall eat; and anyone of his who dies in the open country the birds of the air shall eat.”
text.Rotherham: Him that dieth, of Ahab, in the city, shall, the dogs, eat,—and, him that dieth in the field, shall, the birds of heaven, eat.
text.KJV: 21:24 Him that dieth of Ahab in the citie, the dogs shall eate: and him that dieth in the field, shall the foules of the aire eat.
text.NABRE: Anyone of Ahab’s line who dies in the city, dogs will devour; Anyone who dies in the field, the birds of the sky will devour.

text.NRSV: (Indeed, there was no one like Ahab, who sold himself to do what was evil in the sight of the Lord, urged on by his wife Jezebel.
text.Rotherham: But indeed, there was none like Ahab, who sold himself to do the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—whom Jezebel his wife goaded on;
text.KJV: 21:25 ¶ But there was none like vnto Ahab, which did sell himselfe to worke wickednesse in the sight of the Lord, whom Iezebel his wife stirred vp.
text.NABRE: Indeed, no one gave himself up to the doing of evil in the sight of the Lord as did Ahab, urged on by his wife Jezebel.

text.NRSV: He acted most abominably in going after idols, as the Amorites had done, whom the Lord drove out before the Israelites.)
text.Rotherham: so that he did very abominably, in going after the manufactured gods,—according to all which the Amorites had done, whom Yahweh dispossessed from before the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: 21:26 And hee did very abominably in following Idoles, according to all things as did the Amorites, whom the Lord cast out before the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: He became completely abominable by going after idols, just as the Amorites had done, whom the Lord drove out of the Israelites’ way.

text.NRSV: When Ahab heard those words, he tore his clothes and put sackcloth over his bare flesh; he fasted, lay in the sackcloth, and went about dejectedly.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Ahab heard these words, that he rent his clothes, and put sackcloth upon his flesh, and fasted,—and lay in sackcloth, and went softly.
text.KJV: 21:27 And it came to passe when Ahab heard those wordes, that hee rent his clothes, and put sackecloth vpon his flesh, and fasted, and lay in sackcloth, and went softly.
text.NABRE: When Ahab heard these words, he tore his garments and put on sackcloth over his bare flesh. He fasted, slept in the sackcloth, and went about subdued.

text.NRSV: Then the word of the Lord came to Elijah the Tishbite:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto Elijah the Tishbite, saying:
text.KJV: 21:28 And the word of the Lord came to Eliiah the Tishbite, saying,
text.NABRE: Then the word of the Lord came to Elijah the Tishbite,

text.NRSV: “Have you seen how Ahab has humbled himself before me? Because he has humbled himself before me, I will not bring the disaster in his days; but in his son’s days I will bring the disaster on his house.”
text.Rotherham: Hast thou seen that Ahab hath humbled himself, before me? Because he hath humbled himself before me, I will not bring in the calamity in his days, In the days of his son, will I bring in the calamity upon his house.
text.KJV: 21:29 Seest thou how Ahab humbleth himselfe before mee? because hee humbleth himselfe before mee, I will not bring the euill in his dayes: but in his sonnes dayes will I bring the euill vpon his house.
text.NABRE: Have you seen how Ahab has humbled himself before me? Since he has humbled himself before me, I will not bring the evil in his time. I will bring the evil upon his house in his son’s time.

text.NRSV: For three years Aram and Israel continued without war.
text.Rotherham: And there continued three years without war between Syria and Israel.
text.KJV: 22:1 And they continued three yeeres without warre betweene Syria and Israel.
text.NABRE: Chapter 22 Ahab’s Defeat by Aram. Three years passed without war between Aram and Israel.

text.NRSV: But in the third year King Jehoshaphat of Judah came down to the king of Israel.
text.Rotherham: But it came to pass, in the third year, that Jehoshaphat king of Judah went down unto the king of Israel.
text.KJV: 22:2 And it came to passe on the third yere, that Iehoshaphat the King of Iudah came downe to the king of Israel.
text.NABRE: In the third year, however, King Jehoshaphat of Judah came down to the king of Israel.

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to his servants, “Do you know that Ramoth-gilead belongs to us, yet we are doing nothing to take it out of the hand of the king of Aram?”
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said unto his servants, Know ye that, ours, is Ramoth-gilead,—yet, we, are too idle to take it out of the hand of the king of Syria?
text.KJV: 22:3 (And the king of Israel said vnto his seruants, Know ye that Ramoth in Gilead is ours, and wee be still, and take it not out of the hand of the king of Syria?)
text.NABRE: The king of Israel said to his servants, “Do you not know that Ramoth-gilead is ours and we are doing nothing to take it from the king of Aram?”

text.NRSV: He said to Jehoshaphat, “Will you go with me to battle at Ramoth-gilead?” Jehoshaphat replied to the king of Israel, “I am as you are; my people are as your people, my horses are as your horses.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto Jehoshaphat, Wilt thou go with me to make war upon Ramoth-gilead?
text.KJV: 22:4 And hee said vnto Iehoshaphat, Wilt thou goe with me to battel to Ramoth Gilead? And Iehoshaphat said to the king of Israel, I am as thou art, my people as thy people, my horses as thy horses.
text.NABRE: He asked Jehoshaphat, “Will you come with me to fight against Ramoth-gilead?” Jehoshaphat answered the king of Israel, “You and I are as one, and your people and my people, your horses and my horses as well.”

text.NRSV: But Jehoshaphat also said to the king of Israel, “Inquire first for the word of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Jehoshaphat said unto the king of Israel, I am as thou art, my people are us thy people, my horses as thy horses.
text.KJV: 22:5 And Iehoshaphat said vnto the king of Israel, Enquire, I pray thee, at the word of the Lord to day.
text.NABRE: Prophetic Condemnation. Jehoshaphat also said to the king of Israel, “Seek the word of the Lord at once.”

text.NRSV: Then the king of Israel gathered the prophets together, about four hundred of them, and said to them, “Shall I go to battle against Ramoth-gilead, or shall I refrain?” They said, “Go up; for the Lord will give it into the hand of the king.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Jehoshaphat unto the king of Israel,—Seek, I pray thee, at once, the word of Yahweh. So the king of Israel gathered together the prophets, about four hundred men, and said unto them—Shall I go against Ramoth-gilead, to battle, or shall I forbear? And they said—Go up, that the Lord may deliver it into the hand of the king.
text.KJV: 22:6 Then the king of Israel gathered the prophets together about foure hundred men, and said vnto them, Shall I goe against Ramoth Gilead to battell, or shall I forbeare? And they said, Goe vp, for the Lord shall deliuer it into the hand of the king.
text.NABRE: The king of Israel assembled the prophets, about four hundred of them, and asked, “Shall I go to fight against Ramoth-gilead or shall I refrain?” They said, “Attack. The Lord will give it into the power of the king.”

text.NRSV: But Jehoshaphat said, “Is there no other prophet of the Lord here of whom we may inquire?”
text.Rotherham: Then said Jehoshaphat, Is there not here a prophet of Yahweh, besides,—that we may seek, from him?
text.KJV: 22:7 And Iehoshaphat said, Is there not here a Prophet of the Lord besides, that we might enquire of him?
text.NABRE: But Jehoshaphat said, “Is there no other prophet of the Lord here we might consult?”

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “There is still one other by whom we may inquire of the Lord, Micaiah son of Imlah; but I hate him, for he never prophesies anything favorable about me, but only disaster.” Jehoshaphat said, “Let the king not say such a thing.”
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat, Besides, is a certain man, by whom we might seek Yahweh, but, I, hate him, for he is never moved to prophesy concerning me anything good, only evil, Micaiah, son of Imlah. And Jehoshaphat said, Let not the king say so.
text.KJV: 22:8 And the king of Israel said vnto Iehoshaphat, There is yet one man, (Micaiah the sonne of Imlah) by whom we may enquire of the Lord; but I hate him, for he doth not prophesie good concerning me, but euill. And Iehoshaphat said, Let not the King say so.
text.NABRE: The king of Israel answered, “There is one other man through whom we might consult the Lord; but I hate him because he prophesies not good but evil about me. He is Micaiah, son of Imlah.” Jehoshaphat said, “Let not the king say that.”

text.NRSV: Then the king of Israel summoned an officer and said, “Bring quickly Micaiah son of Imlah.”
text.Rotherham: Then the king of Israel called a certain courtier,—and said, Hasten Micaiah son of Imlah.
text.KJV: 22:9 Then the king of Israel called an Officer, and said, Hasten hither Micaiah the sonne of Imlah.
text.NABRE: So the king of Israel called an official and said to him, “Get Micaiah, son of Imlah, at once.”

text.NRSV: Now the king of Israel and King Jehoshaphat of Judah were sitting on their thrones, arrayed in their robes, at the threshing floor at the entrance of the gate of Samaria; and all the prophets were prophesying before them.
text.Rotherham: Now, the king of Israel, and Jehoshaphat king of Judah, were sitting—each man upon his throne, having put on robes, in a level place, at the entrance of the gate of Samaria,—and, all the prophets, were being moved to prophesy before them;
text.KJV: 22:10 And the King of Israel and Iehoshaphat the King of Iudah sate each on his throne, hauing put on their robes, in a voyd place in the entrance of the gate of Samaria, and all the Prophets prophecied before them.
text.NABRE: The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, were seated, each on his throne, clothed in their robes of state in the square at the entrance of the gate of Samaria, and all the prophets were prophesying before them.

text.NRSV: Zedekiah son of Chenaanah made for himself horns of iron, and he said, “Thus says the Lord: With these you shall gore the Arameans until they are destroyed.”
text.Rotherham: when Zedekiah son of Chenaanah made himself horns of iron,—and said—Thus, saith Yahweh,—With these, shalt thou push down the Syrians, until thou hast consumed them.
text.KJV: 22:11 And Zedekiah the sonne of Chenaanah made him hornes of yron: and he sayd, Thus saith the Lord, With these shalt thou push the Syrians, vntill thou haue consumed them.
text.NABRE: Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, made himself two horns of iron and said, “The Lord says, With these you shall gore Aram until you have destroyed them.”

text.NRSV: All the prophets were prophesying the same and saying, “Go up to Ramoth-gilead and triumph; the Lord will give it into the hand of the king.”
text.Rotherham: And, all the prophets, were being moved to prophesy, in like manner, saying,—Go up to Ramoth-gilead, and thou shalt prosper, and Yahweh will deliver it, into the hand of the king.
text.KJV: 22:12 And all the Prophets prophecied so, saying; Goe vp to Ramoth Gilead, and prosper: for the Lord shall deliuer it into the kings hand.
text.NABRE: The other prophets prophesied in a similar vein, saying: “Attack Ramoth-gilead and conquer! The Lord will give it into the power of the king.”

text.NRSV: The messenger who had gone to summon Micaiah said to him, “Look, the words of the prophets with one accord are favorable to the king; let your word be like the word of one of them, and speak favorably.”
text.Rotherham: Now, the messenger who went to call Micaiah, spake unto him, saying, Behold, I pray thee, the words of the prophets, with one mouth, are good, as touching the king, let thy word, I pray thee, be as the word of one of them, so wilt thou speak that which is good.
text.KJV: 22:13 And the messenger that was gone to call Micaiah, spake vnto him, saying, Behold now, the words of the prophets declare good vnto the King with one mouth: let thy word, I pray thee, bee like the word of one of them, and speake that which is good.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile, the messenger who had gone to call Micaiah said to him, “Look now, the prophets are unanimously predicting good for the king. Let your word be the same as any of theirs; speak a good word.”

text.NRSV: But Micaiah said, “As the Lord lives, whatever the Lord says to me, that I will speak.”
text.Rotherham: And Micaiah said,—By the life of Yahweh, what Yahweh saith unto me, that, will I, speak.
text.KJV: 22:14 And Micaiah sayde, As the Lord liueth, what the Lord saith vnto me, that will I speake.
text.NABRE: Micaiah said, “As the Lord lives, I shall speak whatever the Lord tells me.”

text.NRSV: When he had come to the king, the king said to him, “Micaiah, shall we go to Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall we refrain?” He answered him, “Go up and triumph; the Lord will give it into the hand of the king.”
text.Rotherham: So he came unto the king, and the king said unto him, Micaiah, shall we go against Ramoth-gilead to battle, or shall we forbear? And he said unto him—Go up and prosper, and Yahweh will deliver it into the hand of the king.
text.KJV: 22:15 ¶ So he came to the king, and the king sayd vnto him, Micaiah, shall wee goe against Ramoth Gilead to battell, or shall we forbeare? And he answered him, Go, and prosper: for the Lord shall deliuer it into the hand of the king.
text.NABRE: When he came to the king, the king said to him, “Micaiah, shall we go to fight at Ramoth-gilead, or shall we refrain?” He said, “Attack and conquer! The Lord will give it into the power of the king.”

text.NRSV: But the king said to him, “How many times must I adjure you to tell me nothing but the truth in the name of the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: And the king said unto him, How many times must, I, adjure thee,—that thou speak unto me nothing but truth, in the name of Yahweh?
text.KJV: 22:16 And the king said vnto him, How many times shall I adiure thee, that thou tell me nothing but that which is true, in the Name of the Lord ?
text.NABRE: But the king answered him, “How many times must I adjure you to tell me nothing but the truth in the name of the Lord?”

text.NRSV: Then Micaiah said, “I saw all Israel scattered on the mountains, like sheep that have no shepherd; and the Lord said, ‘These have no master; let each one go home in peace.’”
text.Rotherham: And he said—I saw all Israel, scattered among the mountains, like sheep that have no shepherd,—so Yahweh said, These have, no masters, let them return every man unto his own house, in peace.
text.KJV: 22:17 And hee sayd, I saw all Israel scattered vpon the hilles, as sheepe that haue not a shepheard. And the Lord said, These haue no master, let them returne euery man to his house in peace.
text.NABRE: So Micaiah said: “I see all Israel scattered on the mountains, like sheep without a shepherd, And the Lord saying, These have no master! Let each of them go back home in peace.”

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “Did I not tell you that he would not prophesy anything favorable about me, but only disaster?”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king of Israel, unto Jehoshaphat,—Did I not say unto thee, He will not be moved to prophesy concerning me anything good, only calamity.
text.KJV: 22:18 And the King of Israel said vnto Iehoshaphat, Did I not tell thee, that he would prophecie no good concerning me, but euill?
text.NABRE: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “Did I not tell you, he does not prophesy good about me, but only evil?”

text.NRSV: Then Micaiah said, “Therefore hear the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord sitting on his throne, with all the host of heaven standing beside him to the right and to the left of him.
text.Rotherham: Then he said, Therefore, hear thou the word of Yahweh,—I saw Yahweh, sitting upon his throne, and all the host of the heavens, standing by him, on his right hand, and on his left.
text.KJV: 22:19 And he said, Heare thou therefore the word of the Lord: I sawe the Lord sitting on his Throne, and all the hoste of heauen standing by him, on his right hand and on his left.
text.NABRE: Micaiah continued: “Therefore hear the word of the Lord: I saw the Lord seated on his throne, with the whole host of heaven standing to his right and to his left.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said, ‘Who will entice Ahab, so that he may go up and fall at Ramoth-gilead?’ Then one said one thing, and another said another,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said, Who will persuade Ahab, that he may go up and fall, at Ramoth-gilead? And, one, said in this manner, and, another, said in that manner.
text.KJV: 22:20 And the Lord said, Who shall perswade Ahab, that hee may goe vp and fall at Ramoth Gilead? And one sayd on this manner, and another said on that manner.
text.NABRE: The Lord asked: Who will deceive Ahab, so that he will go up and fall on Ramoth-gilead? And one said this, another that,

text.NRSV: until a spirit came forward and stood before the Lord, saying, ‘I will entice him.’
text.Rotherham: Then came forth a spirit, and stood before Yahweh, and said—I, will persuade him.
text.KJV: 22:21 And there came forth a spirit, and stood before the Lord, and said, I will perswade him.
text.NABRE: until this spirit came forth and stood before the Lord, saying, ‘I will deceive him.’ The Lord asked: How?

text.NRSV: ‘How?’ the Lord asked him. He replied, ‘I will go out and be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets.’ Then the Lord said, ‘You are to entice him, and you shall succeed; go out and do it.’
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto him—Wherewith? And he said—I will go forth, and become a spirit of falsehood in the mouth of all his prophets,—So he said,—Thou mayest persuade, yea and prevail, go forth, and do so.
text.KJV: 22:22 And the Lord said vnto him, Wherewith? And hee sayd, I will goe foorth, and I will be a lying spirit in the mouth of all his prophets. And he said, Thou shalt perswade him, and preuaile also: Goe forth, and doe so.
text.NABRE: He answered, ‘I will go forth and become a lying spirit in the mouths of all his prophets.’ The Lord replied: You shall succeed in deceiving him. Go forth and do this.

text.NRSV: So you see, the Lord has put a lying spirit in the mouth of all these your prophets; the Lord has decreed disaster for you.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! Yahweh hath suffered a spirit of falsehood to be put into the mouth of all these thy prophets. But, Yahweh himself, hath spoken concerning thee, calamity.
text.KJV: 22:23 Now therfore behold, the Lord hath put a lying spirit in the mouth of all these thy prophets, and the Lord hath spoken euill concerning thee.
text.NABRE: So now, the Lord has put a lying spirit in the mouths of all these prophets of yours; the Lord himself has decreed evil against you.”

text.NRSV: Then Zedekiah son of Chenaanah came up to Micaiah, struck him on the cheek, and said, “Which way did the spirit of the Lord go from me to speak to you?”
text.Rotherham: Then drew near Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, and smote Micaiah on the cheek,—and said—Where then passed the Spirit of Yahweh, from me, to speak unto thee?
text.KJV: 22:24 But Zedekiah the sonne of Chenaanah went neere, and smote Micaiah on the cheeke, and said, Which way went the Spirit of the Lord from me, to speake vnto thee?
text.NABRE: Thereupon Zedekiah, son of Chenaanah, came up and struck Micaiah on the cheek, saying, “Has the spirit of the Lord, then, left me to speak with you?”

text.NRSV: Micaiah replied, “You will find out on that day when you go in to hide in an inner chamber.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Micaiah, Lo! thou art about to see, on that day,—when thou enterest a chamber within a chamber, to hide thyself.
text.KJV: 22:25 And Micaiah sayde, Beholde, thou shalt see in that day, when thou shalt goe into an inner chamber, to hide thy selfe.
text.NABRE: Micaiah said, “You shall find out on the day you go into an inner room to hide.”

text.NRSV: The king of Israel then ordered, “Take Micaiah, and return him to Amon the governor of the city and to Joash the king’s son,
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said, Take Micaiah, and carry him back unto Amon captain of the city,—and unto Joash son of the king;
text.KJV: 22:26 And the King of Israel sayde, Take Micaiah, and cary him backe vnto Amon the gouernour of the citie, and to Ioash the kings sonne:
text.NABRE: The king of Israel then said, “Seize Micaiah and take him back to Amon, prefect of the city, and to Joash, the king’s son,

text.NRSV: and say, ‘Thus says the king: Put this fellow in prison, and feed him on reduced rations of bread and water until I come in peace.’”
text.Rotherham: and thou shalt say—Thus, saith the king, Put this man into the prison,—and let him eat the bread of oppression, with the water of oppression, until I enter in peace.
text.KJV: 22:27 And say, Thus sayth the King, Put this fellow in the prison, and feede him with bread of affliction, and with water of afflictiō, vntil I come in peace.
text.NABRE: and say, ‘This is the king’s order: Put this man in prison and feed him scanty rations of bread and water until I come back in safety.’”

text.NRSV: Micaiah said, “If you return in peace, the Lord has not spoken by me.” And he said, “Hear, you peoples, all of you!”
text.Rotherham: Then said Micaiah, If thou, return, in peace, Yahweh hath not spoken by me. And he said, Hear, ye peoples, all of you!
text.KJV: 22:28 And Micaiah saide, If thou returne at all in peace, the Lord hath not spoken by me. And he said, Hearken, O people, euery one of you.
text.NABRE: But Micaiah said, “If you return in safety, the Lord has not spoken through me.” (He also said, “Hear, O peoples, all of you.”)

text.NRSV: So the king of Israel and King Jehoshaphat of Judah went up to Ramoth-gilead.
text.Rotherham: So the king of Israel went up, with Jehoshaphat king of Judah, unto Ramoth-gilead.
text.KJV: 22:29 So the King of Israel, and Iehoshaphat the king of Iudah, went vp to Ramoth Gilead.
text.NABRE: Ahab at Ramoth-gilead. The king of Israel and Jehoshaphat, king of Judah, went up to Ramoth-gilead,

text.NRSV: The king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “I will disguise myself and go into battle, but you wear your robes.” So the king of Israel disguised himself and went into the battle.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Israel said unto Jehoshaphat—I am about to disguise myself, and enter into the battle, thou, therefore, put on thy robes. So the king of Israel disguised himself, and entered into the battle.
text.KJV: 22:30 And the king of Israel said vnto Iehoshaphat, I wil disguise my selfe, & enter into the battell, but put thou on thy robes. And the King of Israel disguised himselfe, & went into the battell.
text.NABRE: and the king of Israel said to Jehoshaphat, “I will disguise myself and go into battle, but you put on your own robes.” So the king of Israel disguised himself and entered the battle.

text.NRSV: Now the king of Aram had commanded the thirty-two captains of his chariots, “Fight with no one small or great, but only with the king of Israel.”
text.Rotherham: Now, the king of Syria, had commanded the captains of chariots which he had, thirty and two, saying, Ye shall not fight with small or great,—save with the king of Israel alone.
text.KJV: 22:31 But the King of Syria commanded his thirtie and two Captaines that had rule ouer his charets, saying, Fight neither with small nor great, saue only with the king of Israel.
text.NABRE: In the meantime the king of Aram had given his thirty-two chariot commanders the order, “Do not fight with anyone, great or small, except the king of Israel alone.”

text.NRSV: When the captains of the chariots saw Jehoshaphat, they said, “It is surely the king of Israel.” So they turned to fight against him; and Jehoshaphat cried out.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the chariot-captains saw Jehoshaphat, that, they, said: Surely it is, the king of Israel! But, when they turned aside against him to fight, Jehoshaphat cried out.
text.KJV: 22:32 And it came to passe, when the captaines of the charets saw Iehoshaphat, that they said, Surely it is the king of Israel. And they turned aside to fight against him: and Iehoshaphat cryed out.
text.NABRE: When the chariot commanders saw Jehoshaphat, they cried out, “There is the king of Israel!” and wheeled to fight him. But Jehoshaphat cried out,

text.NRSV: When the captains of the chariots saw that it was not the king of Israel, they turned back from pursuing him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the chariot-captains saw that it was, not the king of Israel, that they turned back, from pursuing him.
text.KJV: 22:33 And it came to passe, when the captaines of the charets perceiued that it was not the king of Israel, that they turned backe from pursuing him.
text.NABRE: and the chariot commanders, seeing that he was not the king of Israel, turned away from him.

text.NRSV: But a certain man drew his bow and unknowingly struck the king of Israel between the scale armor and the breastplate; so he said to the driver of his chariot, “Turn around, and carry me out of the battle, for I am wounded.”
text.Rotherham: But, a certain man, drawing a bow in his innocence, smote the king of Israel, between the shoulder-joints and the coat of mail,—wherefore he said to his charioteer—Turn thy hand, and convey me out of the host, for I am sore wounded.
text.KJV: 22:34 And a certaine man drew a bow at a venture, and smote the king of Israel betweene the ioynts of the harnesse: wherefore hee sayd vnto the driuer of his charet, Turne thine hand, and cary me out of the hoste, for I am wounded.
text.NABRE: But someone drew his bow at random, and hit the king of Israel between the joints of his breastplate. He ordered his charioteer, “Rein about and take me out of the ranks, for I am wounded.”

text.NRSV: The battle raged that day, and the king was propped up in his chariot facing the Arameans, until at evening he died; the blood from the wound had flowed into the bottom of the chariot.
text.Rotherham: But the battle increased that day, and, the king, was propped up in the chariot, before the Syrians,—and died in the evening, and so the blood of the wound ran out into the hollow of the chariot.
text.KJV: 22:35 And the battell increased that day: and the king was stayed vp in his charet against the Syrians, and died at euen: and the blood ranne out of the wound, into the mids of the charet.
text.NABRE: The battle grew fierce during the day, and the king, who was propped up in his chariot facing the Arameans, died in the evening. The blood from his wound flowed to the bottom of the chariot.

text.NRSV: Then about sunset a shout went through the army, “Every man to his city, and every man to his country!”
text.Rotherham: And a loud cry went through the host, at the going in of the sun, saying—Every man to his own city! and every man to his own land!
text.KJV: 22:36 And there went a proclamation throughout the hoste, about the going downe of the Sunne, saying, Euery man to his citie, and euery man to his owne countrey.
text.NABRE: At sunset a cry went through the army, “Every man to his city, every man to his land!”

text.NRSV: So the king died, and was brought to Samaria; they buried the king in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: So the king died, and was brought into Samaria,—and they buried the king, in Samaria.
text.KJV: 22:37 ¶ So the King died, and was brought to Samaria, and they buried the king in Samaria.
text.NABRE: And so the king died, and came back to Samaria, and they buried him there.

text.NRSV: They washed the chariot by the pool of Samaria; the dogs licked up his blood, and the prostitutes washed themselves in it, according to the word of the Lord that he had spoken.
text.Rotherham: And, when the chariot was washed out at the pool of Samaria, the dogs lapped up his blood, also, the harlots, bathed [there],—according to the word of Yahweh which he had spoken.
text.KJV: 22:38 And one washed the charet in the poole of Samaria, and the dogges licked vp his blood, and they washed his armour, according vnto the word of the Lord which he spake.
text.NABRE: When they washed out the chariot at the pool of Samaria, the dogs licked up his blood and prostitutes bathed there, as the Lord had prophesied.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Ahab, and all that he did, and the ivory house that he built, and all the cities that he built, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Ahab, and all that he did, and the house of ivory that he built, and all the cities that he built, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.KJV: 22:39 Now the rest of the actes of Ahab, and all that he did, and the Iuory house which he made, and all the cities that he built, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Ahab, with all that he did, including the ivory house he built and all the cities he built, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: So Ahab slept with his ancestors; and his son Ahaziah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So Ahab slept with his fathers,—and, Ahaziah his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: 22:40 So Ahab slept with his fathers, and Ahaziah his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Ahab rested with his ancestors, and his son Ahaziah succeeded him as king.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat son of Asa began to reign over Judah in the fourth year of King Ahab of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, Jehoshaphat son of Asa, began to reign over Judah,—in the fourth year of Ahab, king of Israel.
text.KJV: 22:41 ¶ And Iehoshaphat the sonne of Asa began to reigne ouer Iudah in the fourth yeere of Ahab King of Israel.
text.NABRE: Reign of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat, son of Asa, became king of Judah in the fourth year of Ahab, king of Israel.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat was thirty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Azubah daughter of Shilhi.
text.Rotherham: Jehoshaphat, was thirty-five years old when he began to reign, and, twenty-five years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, the name of his mother, was Azubah, daughter of Shilhi.
text.KJV: 22:42 Iehoshaphat was thirtie and fiue yeeres olde when hee began to reigne, and he reigned twentie and fiue yeeres in Ierusalem: and his mothers name was Azubah the daughter of Shilhi.
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat was thirty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned twenty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Azubah, daughter of Shilhi.

text.NRSV: He walked in all the way of his father Asa; he did not turn aside from it, doing what was right in the sight of the Lord; yet the high places were not taken away, and the people still sacrificed and offered incense on the high places.
text.Rotherham: And he walked in all the way of Asa his father, he turned not aside therefrom,—doing that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh: nevertheless, the high places, were not taken away,—still were the people offering sacrifices and burning incense in the high places.
text.KJV: 22:43 And he walked in all the wayes of Asa his father, hee turned not aside from it, doing that which was right in the eyes of the Lord: neuerthelesse, the high places were not taken away: for the people offered and burnt incense yet in the high places.
text.NABRE: He walked in the way of Asa his father unceasingly, doing what was right in the Lord ’s sight.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat also made peace with the king of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoshaphat made peace with the king of Israel.
text.KJV: 22:44 And Iehoshaphat made peace with the king of Israel.
text.NABRE: Nevertheless, the high places did not disappear, and the people still sacrificed on the high places and burned incense there.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, and his power that he showed, and how he warred, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jehoshaphat, and his might that he shewed, and how he warred, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.KJV: 22:45 Now the rest of the actes of Iehoshaphat, and his might that hee shewed, and how he warred, are they not written in the booke of the Chronicles of the Kings of Iudah?
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat also made peace with the king of Israel.

text.NRSV: The remnant of the male temple prostitutes who were still left in the days of his father Asa, he exterminated from the land.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, the rest of the male devotees who remained in the days of Asa his father, he consumed out of the land.
text.KJV: 22:46 And the remnant of the Sodomites which remained in the dayes of his father Asa, he tooke out of the land.
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Jehoshaphat, with his valor, what he did and how he fought, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.

text.NRSV: There was no king in Edom; a deputy was king.
text.Rotherham: And, king, was there none in Edom, a prefect, was king.
text.KJV: 22:47 There was then no king in Edom: a deputie was king.
text.NABRE: He removed from the land the rest of the pagan priests who had remained in the reign of Asa his father.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat made ships of Tarshish to go to Ophir for gold; but they did not go, for the ships were wrecked at Ezion-geber.
text.Rotherham: Jehoshaphat, made ships of Tarshish, to go to Ophir, for gold; but they went not,—for ships had been broken to pieces in Ezion-geber.
text.KJV: 22:48 Iehoshaphat made shippes of Tharshish to goe to Ophir for golde: but they went not, for the shippes were broken at Ezion Geber.
text.NABRE: There was no king in Edom, but an appointed regent.

text.NRSV: Then Ahaziah son of Ahab said to Jehoshaphat, “Let my servants go with your servants in the ships,” but Jehoshaphat was not willing.
text.Rotherham: Then, said Ahaziah son of Ahab, unto Jehoshaphat, Let, my servants, go with, thy servants, in the ships,—but Jehoshaphat did not consent.
text.KJV: 22:49 Then said Ahaziah the sonne of Ahab vnto Iehoshaphat, Let my seruants goe with thy seruants in the ships: But Iehoshaphat would not.
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat made Tarshish ships to go to Ophir for gold; but in fact the ships did not go, because they were wrecked at Ezion-geber.

text.NRSV: Jehoshaphat slept with his ancestors and was buried with his ancestors in the city of his father David; his son Jehoram succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So Jehoshaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers, in the city of David his father,—and, Jehoram his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: 22:50 ¶ And Iehoshaphat slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the citie of Dauid his father: and Iehoram his sonne reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: That was the time when Ahaziah, son of Ahab, had said to Jehoshaphat, “Let my servants accompany your servants in the ships.” But Jehoshaphat would not agree.

text.NRSV: Ahaziah son of Ahab began to reign over Israel in Samaria in the seventeenth year of King Jehoshaphat of Judah; he reigned two years over Israel.
text.Rotherham: Ahaziah son of Ahab, began to reign over Israel, in Samaria, in the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat king of Judah,—and reigned over Israel, two years.
text.KJV: 22:51 ¶ Ahaziah the sonne of Ahab began to reigne ouer Israel in Samaria the seuenteenth yeere of Iehoshaphat king of Iudah, and reigned two yeres ouer Israel.
text.NABRE: Jehoshaphat rested with his ancestors; he was buried with his ancestors in the City of David his father, and his son Jehoram succeeded him as king. Reign of Ahaziah.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, and walked in the way of his father and mother, and in the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—and went in the way of his father, and in the way of his mother, and in the way of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who caused, Israel, to sin.
text.KJV: 22:52 And he did euill in the sight of the Lord, and walked in the way of his father, and in the way of his mother, and in the way of Ieroboam the sonne of Nebat, who made Israel to sinne.
text.NABRE: Ahaziah, son of Ahab, became king over Israel in Samaria in the seventeenth year of Jehoshaphat, king of Judah; he reigned two years over Israel.

text.NRSV: He served Baal and worshiped him, and provoked the Lord, the God of Israel, to anger, just as his father had done.
text.Rotherham: Yea he served Baal, and bowed down to him,—and provoked to anger Yahweh, God of Israel, according to all that his father had done.
text.KJV: 22:53 For he serued Baal, and worshipped him, and prouoked to anger the Lord God of Israel, according vnto all that his father had done.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, walking in the way of his father, his mother, and Jeroboam, son of Nebat, who caused Israel to sin.
